easypaperless

easypaperless — Python API wrapper for paperless-ngx.

 1"""easypaperless — Python API wrapper for paperless-ngx."""
 2
 3import logging
 4
 5from easypaperless.client import PaperlessClient
 6from easypaperless.exceptions import (
 7    AuthError,
 8    NotFoundError,
 9    PaperlessError,
10    ServerError,
11    TaskTimeoutError,
12    UploadError,
13    ValidationError,
14)
15from easypaperless.models import (
16    Correspondent,
17    CustomField,
18    CustomFieldValue,
19    Document,
20    DocumentMetadata,
21    DocumentNote,
22    DocumentType,
23    FieldDataType,
24    FileMetadataEntry,
25    SearchHit,
26    StoragePath,
27    Tag,
28    Task,
29    TaskStatus,
30)
31from easypaperless._embedding import OllamaProvider, SentenceTransformerProvider
32from easypaperless.store import DocumentStore
33from easypaperless.sync import SyncPaperlessClient
34
35logging.getLogger("easypaperless").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
36

38    # Clients
39    "PaperlessClient",
40    "SyncPaperlessClient",
41    "DocumentStore",
42    # Embedding providers
43    "OllamaProvider",
44    "SentenceTransformerProvider",

47    "CustomField",

68]
class PaperlessClient: 
  40class PaperlessClient:
  41    """Async client for the paperless-ngx API.
  42
  43    All operations are flat methods on the client.  String names are resolved
  44    to integer IDs automatically wherever the API requires IDs (tags,
  45    correspondents, document types, storage paths).
  46
  47    Use as an async context manager to ensure the underlying HTTP connection
  48    pool is closed when you are done:
  49
  50    Example:
  51        async with PaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client:
  52            docs = await client.list_documents()
  53    """
  54
  55    def __init__(
  56        self,
  57        url: str,
  58        api_key: str,
  59        *,
  60        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
  61        poll_timeout: float = 60.0,
  62    ) -> None:
  63        """Create an async paperless-ngx client.
  64
  65        Args:
  66            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
  67                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
  68            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
  69                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
  70            poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when ``wait=True``
  71                is passed to :meth:`upload_document`.  Default: ``2.0``.
  72            poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish
  73                processing before raising
  74                :exc:`~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError`.
  75                Default: ``60.0``.
  76        """
  77        self._session = HttpSession(base_url=url, api_key=api_key)
  78        self._resolver = NameResolver(self._session)
  79        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
  80        self._poll_timeout = poll_timeout
  81
  82    async def close(self) -> None:
  83        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.
  84
  85        Called automatically when used as an async context manager.
  86        """
  87        await self._session.close()
  88
  89    async def __aenter__(self) -> PaperlessClient:
  90        return self
  91
  92    async def __aexit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
  93        await self.close()
  94
  95    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
  96    # Documents
  97    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
  98
  99    async def get_document(self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document:
 100        """Fetch a single document by its ID.
 101
 102        Args:
 103            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
 104            include_metadata: When ``True``, the extended file-level metadata
 105                (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from
 106                ``/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and attached to
 107                :attr:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata`.
 108                Default: ``False``.
 109
 110        Returns:
 111            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document` with the
 112            given ID.  When ``include_metadata=True`` the ``metadata``
 113            attribute is populated; otherwise it is ``None``.
 114
 115        Raises:
 116            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 117                with that ID.
 118        """
 119        if include_metadata:
 120            doc_resp, meta_resp = await asyncio.gather(
 121                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/"),
 122                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/"),
 123            )
 124            data = doc_resp.json()
 125            data["metadata"] = meta_resp.json()
 126        else:
 127            resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/")
 128            data = resp.json()
 129        return Document.model_validate(data)
 130
 131    async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata:
 132        """Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.
 133
 134        This is a lower-overhead alternative to
 135        ``get_document(id, include_metadata=True)`` when you only need the
 136        metadata and not the full document object.
 137
 138        Args:
 139            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
 140
 141        Returns:
 142            A :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata`
 143            instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded
 144            file metadata.
 145
 146        Raises:
 147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 148                with that ID.
 149        """
 150        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/")
 151        return DocumentMetadata.model_validate(resp.json())
 152
 153    async def list_documents(
 154        self,
 155        *,
 156        search: str | None = None,
 157        search_mode: str = "title_or_text",
 158        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 159        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
 160        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 161        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 162        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
 163        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
 164        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 165        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
 166        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
 167        asn: int | None = None,
 168        created_after: str | None = None,
 169        created_before: str | None = None,
 170        added_after: str | None = None,
 171        added_before: str | None = None,
 172        modified_after: str | None = None,
 173        modified_before: str | None = None,
 174        checksum: str | None = None,
 175        page_size: int = 25,
 176        page: int | None = None,
 177        ordering: str | None = None,
 178        descending: bool = False,
 179        max_results: int | None = None,
 180        on_page: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None,
 181    ) -> list[Document]:
 182        """Return a filtered list of documents.
 183
 184        All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either
 185        integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.
 186
 187        Args:
 188            search: Search string.  Behaviour depends on ``search_mode``.
 189            search_mode: How ``search`` is applied.  One of:
 190
 191                * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
 192                  title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
 193                  parameter).
 194                * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
 195                  only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
 196                * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
 197                  ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
 198                * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
 199                  on the original file name (raw API
 200                  ``original_filename__icontains``).
 201
 202            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
 203                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 204            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
 205                (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 206            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
 207                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
 208            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
 209                correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
 210            any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
 211                these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
 212                Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
 213            exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
 214                these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
 215            document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
 216                Accepts a document-type ID or name.
 217            any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
 218                these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
 219                precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
 220            exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
 221                these.  Accepts IDs or names.
 222            asn: Filter by archive serial number.
 223            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 224                documents created **after** this date are returned.
 225            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 226                documents created **before** this date are returned.
 227            added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 228                documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
 229            added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 230                documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
 231            modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 232                documents **modified** after this date are returned.
 233            modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
 234                documents **modified** before this date are returned.
 235            checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
 236                Returns only the document whose original file matches this
 237                checksum.
 238            page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
 239                Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
 240                result sets.
 241            page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
 242                set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
 243                that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
 244                to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
 245                all pages).
 246            ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
 247                ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
 248                control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
 249            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 250                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 251                Default: ``False``.
 252            max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
 253                been collected and return at most this many results.
 254                ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.
 255            on_page: Optional callback invoked after each API page is fetched.
 256                Receives ``(fetched_so_far, total)`` where ``total`` is the
 257                server-reported document count from the first page response
 258                (``None`` if not reported).  Ignored when ``page`` is set.
 259
 260        Returns:
 261            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
 262            objects.
 263        """
 264        params: dict[str, Any] = {"page_size": page_size}
 265
 266        if search is not None:
 267            api_param = _SEARCH_MODE_MAP.get(search_mode, "search")
 268            params[api_param] = search
 269
 270        if tags is not None:
 271            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 272            params["tags__id__all"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 273
 274        if any_tag is not None:
 275            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", any_tag)
 276            params["tags__id__in"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 277
 278        if exclude_tags is not None:
 279            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", exclude_tags)
 280            params["tags__id__none"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
 281
 282        if any_correspondent is not None:
 283            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", any_correspondent)
 284            params["correspondent__id__in"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
 285        elif correspondent is not None:
 286            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 287            params["correspondent__id__in"] = resolved_id
 288
 289        if exclude_correspondents is not None:
 290            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", exclude_correspondents)
 291            params["correspondent__id__none"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
 292
 293        if any_document_type is not None:
 294            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", any_document_type)
 295            params["document_type__id__in"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
 296        elif document_type is not None:
 297            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 298            params["document_type"] = resolved_id
 299
 300        if exclude_document_types is not None:
 301            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", exclude_document_types)
 302            params["document_type__id__none"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
 303
 304        if asn is not None:
 305            params["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 306
 307        if created_after is not None:
 308            params["created__date__gt"] = created_after
 309
 310        if created_before is not None:
 311            params["created__date__lt"] = created_before
 312
 313        if added_after is not None:
 314            params["added__date__gt"] = added_after
 315
 316        if added_before is not None:
 317            params["added__date__lt"] = added_before
 318
 319        if modified_after is not None:
 320            params["modified__date__gt"] = modified_after
 321
 322        if modified_before is not None:
 323            params["modified__date__lt"] = modified_before
 324
 325        if checksum is not None:
 326            params["checksum"] = checksum
 327
 328        if ordering is not None:
 329            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 330
 331        if page is not None:
 332            params["page"] = page
 333            resp = await self._session.get("/documents/", params=params)
 334            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
 335            if max_results is not None:
 336                items = items[:max_results]
 337            return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 338
 339        items = await self._session.get_all_pages(
 340            "/documents/", params, max_results=max_results, on_page=on_page
 341        )
 342        return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 343
 344    async def update_document(
 345        self,
 346        id: int,
 347        *,
 348        title: str | None = None,
 349        date: str | None = None,
 350        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 351        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 352        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
 353        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 354        asn: int | None = None,
 355        custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None,
 356    ) -> Document:
 357        """Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).
 358
 359        Args:
 360            id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
 361            title: New document title.
 362            date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
 363            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
 364                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 365            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
 366                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 367            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
 368                Pass ``0`` to clear.
 369            tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).  The existing
 370                tag list is replaced, not merged.
 371            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
 372            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}``
 373                dicts.  Replaces the existing custom-field values.
 374
 375        Returns:
 376            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
 377        """
 378        logger.debug("Updating document id=%d", id)
 379        payload: dict[str, Any] = {}
 380
 381        if title is not None:
 382            payload["title"] = title
 383        if date is not None:
 384            payload["created"] = date
 385        if correspondent is not None:
 386            payload["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 387        if document_type is not None:
 388            payload["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 389        if storage_path is not None:
 390            payload["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
 391        if tags is not None:
 392            payload["tags"] = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 393        if asn is not None:
 394            payload["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 395        if custom_fields is not None:
 396            payload["custom_fields"] = custom_fields
 397
 398        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/documents/{id}/", json=payload)
 399        return Document.model_validate(resp.json())
 400
 401    async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None:
 402        """Permanently delete a document.
 403
 404        Args:
 405            id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
 406
 407        Raises:
 408            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 409                with that ID.
 410        """
 411        logger.debug("Deleting document id=%d", id)
 412        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{id}/")
 413
 414    async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes:
 415        """Download the binary content of a document.
 416
 417        Args:
 418            id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
 419            original: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns the archived
 420                (post-processed) PDF.  If ``True``, returns the original
 421                file that was uploaded.
 422
 423        Returns:
 424            Raw file bytes.
 425        """
 426        endpoint = "download" if original else "archive"
 427        resp = await self._session.get_download(f"/documents/{id}/{endpoint}/")
 428        content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "")
 429        if "text/html" in content_type or resp.content[:9].lower().startswith(b"<!doctype"):
 430            raise ServerError(
 431                f"Download returned an HTML page (content-type: {content_type!r}). "
 432                "The server redirected to a login page even after re-attaching auth. "
 433                "Run with --verbose to see redirect details.",
 434                status_code=None,
 435            )
 436        return resp.content
 437
 438    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 439    # Notes
 440    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 441
 442    async def get_notes(self, document_id: int) -> list[DocumentNote]:
 443        """Fetch all notes attached to a document.
 444
 445        Args:
 446            document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.
 447
 448        Returns:
 449            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote` objects,
 450            ordered by creation time.
 451
 452        Raises:
 453            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 454                with that ID.
 455        """
 456        logger.debug("Fetching notes for document id=%d", document_id)
 457        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/")
 458        return [DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in resp.json()]
 459
 460    async def create_note(self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> DocumentNote:
 461        """Create a new note on a document.
 462
 463        Args:
 464            document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate.
 465            note: Text content of the note.
 466
 467        Returns:
 468            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote`.
 469
 470        Raises:
 471            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 472                with that ID.
 473        """
 474        logger.debug("Creating note for document id=%d", document_id)
 475        resp = await self._session.post(
 476            f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/",
 477            json={"note": note},
 478        )
 479        # paperless-ngx returns the full list of notes after creation;
 480        # the newly created note is the last item in the list.
 481        data = resp.json()
 482        if isinstance(data, list):
 483            return DocumentNote.model_validate(data[-1])
 484        return DocumentNote.model_validate(data)
 485
 486    async def delete_note(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None:
 487        """Delete a note from a document.
 488
 489        Args:
 490            document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note.
 491            note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.
 492
 493        Raises:
 494            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note
 495                exists with the given IDs.
 496        """
 497        logger.debug("Deleting note id=%d from document id=%d", note_id, document_id)
 498        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/", params={"id": note_id})
 499
 500    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 501    # Upload
 502    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 503
 504    async def upload_document(
 505        self,
 506        file: str | Path,
 507        *,
 508        title: str | None = None,
 509        created: str | None = None,
 510        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
 511        document_type: int | str | None = None,
 512        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
 513        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 514        asn: int | None = None,
 515        wait: bool = False,
 516        poll_interval: float | None = None,
 517        poll_timeout: float | None = None,
 518    ) -> str | Document:
 519        """Upload a document to paperless-ngx.
 520
 521        Args:
 522            file: Path to the file to upload (``str`` or
 523                :class:`~pathlib.Path`).
 524            title: Title to assign to the document.  Paperless-ngx derives
 525                one from the file name if omitted.
 526            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
 527            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
 528            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
 529            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
 530            tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
 531            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
 532            wait: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns immediately with the
 533                task ID string.  If ``True``, polls the task-status endpoint
 534                until processing completes and returns the resulting
 535                :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
 536            poll_interval: Override the client-level ``poll_interval`` for
 537                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
 538            poll_timeout: Override the client-level ``poll_timeout`` for
 539                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
 540
 541        Returns:
 542            The Celery task ID string when ``wait=False``, or the fully
 543            processed :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
 544            when ``wait=True``.
 545
 546        Raises:
 547            ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports
 548                that the processing task failed.
 549            ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If ``wait=True`` and
 550                processing does not complete within the timeout.
 551        """
 552        file_path = Path(file)
 553        file_bytes = file_path.read_bytes()
 554        logger.info("Uploading %r (%d bytes)", file_path.name, len(file_bytes))
 555
 556        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
 557        if title is not None:
 558            data["title"] = title
 559        if created is not None:
 560            data["created"] = created
 561        if correspondent is not None:
 562            data["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
 563        if document_type is not None:
 564            data["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
 565        if storage_path is not None:
 566            data["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
 567        if tags is not None:
 568            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
 569            data["tags"] = resolved
 570        if asn is not None:
 571            data["archive_serial_number"] = asn
 572
 573        files = {"document": (file_path.name, file_bytes)}
 574        resp = await self._session.post("/documents/post_document/", data=data, files=files)
 575        task_id: str = resp.text.strip('"')
 576        logger.debug("Upload accepted, task_id=%r", task_id)
 577
 578        if not wait:
 579            return task_id
 580
 581        interval = poll_interval if poll_interval is not None else self._poll_interval
 582        timeout = poll_timeout if poll_timeout is not None else self._poll_timeout
 583        return await self._poll_task(task_id, poll_interval=interval, poll_timeout=timeout)
 584
 585    async def _poll_task(
 586        self, task_id: str, *, poll_interval: float, poll_timeout: float
 587    ) -> Document:
 588        start = time.monotonic()
 589        deadline = start + poll_timeout
 590        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
 591            resp = await self._session.get("/tasks/", params={"task_id": task_id})
 592            tasks = resp.json()
 593            if not tasks:
 594                await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
 595                continue
 596
 597            task = Task.model_validate(tasks[0])
 598            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
 599            logger.debug("Polling task %r (status=%s, elapsed=%.1fs)", task_id, task.status.value, elapsed)
 600            if task.status == TaskStatus.SUCCESS:
 601                doc_id = int(task.related_document)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
 602                logger.info("Task %r succeeded, document_id=%d", task_id, doc_id)
 603                return await self.get_document(doc_id)
 604            elif task.status == TaskStatus.FAILURE:
 605                logger.warning("Task %r failed: %s", task_id, task.result)
 606                raise UploadError(f"Document processing failed: {task.result}")
 607            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
 608
 609        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
 610        logger.warning("Task %r timed out after %.1fs", task_id, elapsed)
 611        raise TaskTimeoutError(f"Task {task_id!r} did not complete within {poll_timeout}s")
 612
 613    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 614    # Bulk operations
 615    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 616
 617    async def bulk_edit(
 618        self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any
 619    ) -> None:
 620        """Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.
 621
 622        This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as
 623        :meth:`bulk_add_tag`, :meth:`bulk_remove_tag`, and
 624        :meth:`bulk_modify_tags`.
 625
 626        Args:
 627            document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on.
 628            method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx
 629                (e.g. ``"add_tag"``, ``"remove_tag"``, ``"delete"``).
 630            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as
 631                the ``parameters`` object.
 632        """
 633        payload = {"documents": document_ids, "method": method, "parameters": parameters}
 634        await self._session.post("/documents/bulk_edit/", json=payload)
 635
 636    async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
 637        """Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.
 638
 639        Args:
 640            document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
 641            tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
 642        """
 643        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
 644        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "add_tag", tag=tag_id)
 645
 646    async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
 647        """Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.
 648
 649        Args:
 650            document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag.
 651            tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
 652        """
 653        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
 654        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "remove_tag", tag=tag_id)
 655
 656    async def bulk_modify_tags(
 657        self,
 658        document_ids: list[int],
 659        *,
 660        add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 661        remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
 662    ) -> None:
 663        """Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.
 664
 665        Args:
 666            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
 667            add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
 668            remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.
 669        """
 670        add_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", add_tags or [])
 671        remove_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", remove_tags or [])
 672        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "modify_tags", add_tags=add_ids, remove_tags=remove_ids)
 673
 674    async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None:
 675        """Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.
 676
 677        Args:
 678            document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.
 679        """
 680        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "delete")
 681
 682    async def bulk_edit_objects(
 683        self,
 684        object_type: str,
 685        object_ids: list[int],
 686        operation: str,
 687        **parameters: Any,
 688    ) -> None:
 689        """Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).
 690
 691        Args:
 692            object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g.
 693                ``"tags"``, ``"correspondents"``).
 694            object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on.
 695            operation: Operation name recognised by the
 696                ``/bulk_edit_objects/`` endpoint.
 697            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to
 698                the API payload.
 699        """
 700        payload = {
 701            "objects": object_ids,
 702            "object_type": object_type,
 703            "operation": operation,
 704            **parameters,
 705        }
 706        await self._session.post("/bulk_edit_objects/", json=payload)
 707
 708    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 709    # Internal resource helpers
 710    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 711
 712    async def _list_resource(
 713        self,
 714        resource: str,
 715        model_class: type,
 716        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 717    ) -> list:
 718        if params and "page" in params:
 719            resp = await self._session.get(f"/{resource}/", params=params)
 720            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
 721        else:
 722            items = await self._session.get_all_pages(f"/{resource}/", params)
 723        return [model_class.model_validate(item) for item in items]
 724
 725    async def _get_resource(self, resource: str, id: int, model_class: type):
 726        resp = await self._session.get(f"/{resource}/{id}/")
 727        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 728
 729    async def _create_resource(self, resource: str, model_class: type, **kwargs: Any):
 730        logger.debug("Creating %s resource", resource)
 731        payload = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if v is not None}
 732        resp = await self._session.post(f"/{resource}/", json=payload)
 733        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 734        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 735
 736    async def _update_resource(self, resource: str, id: int, model_class: type, **kwargs: Any):
 737        logger.debug("Updating %s resource id=%d", resource, id)
 738        payload = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if v is not None}
 739        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/{resource}/{id}/", json=payload)
 740        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 741        return model_class.model_validate(resp.json())
 742
 743    async def _delete_resource(self, resource: str, id: int) -> None:
 744        logger.debug("Deleting %s resource id=%d", resource, id)
 745        await self._session.delete(f"/{resource}/{id}/")
 746        self._resolver.invalidate(resource)
 747
 748    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 749    # Tags
 750    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 751
 752    async def list_tags(
 753        self,
 754        *,
 755        ids: list[int] | None = None,
 756        name_contains: str | None = None,
 757        page: int | None = None,
 758        page_size: int | None = None,
 759        ordering: str | None = None,
 760        descending: bool = False,
 761    ) -> list[Tag]:
 762        """Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.
 763
 764        Args:
 765            ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
 766            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name
 767                (raw API ``name__icontains``).
 768            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
 769                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
 770            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
 771                default is used.
 772            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
 773            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 774                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 775
 776        Returns:
 777            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` objects.
 778        """
 779        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 780        if ids is not None:
 781            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 782        if name_contains is not None:
 783            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 784        if page is not None:
 785            params["page"] = page
 786        if page_size is not None:
 787            params["page_size"] = page_size
 788        if ordering is not None:
 789            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 790        return await self._list_resource("tags", Tag, params or None)
 791
 792    async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag:
 793        """Fetch a single tag by its ID.
 794
 795        Args:
 796            id: Numeric tag ID.
 797
 798        Returns:
 799            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` with the given ID.
 800
 801        Raises:
 802            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
 803                that ID.
 804        """
 805        return await self._get_resource("tags", id, Tag)
 806
 807    async def create_tag(
 808        self,
 809        *,
 810        name: str,
 811        color: str | None = None,
 812        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
 813        match: str | None = None,
 814        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 815        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 816        parent: int | None = None,
 817    ) -> Tag:
 818        """Create a new tag.
 819
 820        Args:
 821            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
 822            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
 823                string (e.g. ``"#ff0000"``).
 824            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
 825                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
 826                the inbox tag.
 827            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 828                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 829            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 830                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 831                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 832            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 833                case-insensitively.
 834            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
 835                hierarchical tag trees. ``None`` creates a root-level tag.
 836
 837        Returns:
 838            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
 839        """
 840        return await self._create_resource(
 841            "tags",
 842            Tag,
 843            name=name,
 844            color=color,
 845            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
 846            match=match,
 847            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 848            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 849            parent=parent,
 850        )
 851
 852    async def update_tag(
 853        self,
 854        id: int,
 855        *,
 856        name: str | None = None,
 857        color: str | None = None,
 858        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
 859        match: str | None = None,
 860        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 861        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 862        parent: int | None = None,
 863    ) -> Tag:
 864        """Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).
 865
 866        Args:
 867            id: Numeric ID of the tag to update.
 868            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
 869            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
 870                string (e.g. ``"#00ff00"``).
 871            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
 872                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
 873                the inbox tag.
 874            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 875                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 876            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 877                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 878                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 879            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 880                case-insensitively.
 881            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
 882                hierarchical tag trees.
 883
 884        Returns:
 885            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
 886        """
 887        return await self._update_resource(
 888            "tags",
 889            id,
 890            Tag,
 891            name=name,
 892            color=color,
 893            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
 894            match=match,
 895            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
 896            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
 897            parent=parent,
 898        )
 899
 900    async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None:
 901        """Delete a tag.
 902
 903        Args:
 904            id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.
 905
 906        Raises:
 907            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
 908                that ID.
 909        """
 910        await self._delete_resource("tags", id)
 911
 912    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 913    # Correspondents
 914    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 915
 916    async def list_correspondents(
 917        self,
 918        *,
 919        ids: list[int] | None = None,
 920        name_contains: str | None = None,
 921        page: int | None = None,
 922        page_size: int | None = None,
 923        ordering: str | None = None,
 924        descending: bool = False,
 925    ) -> list[Correspondent]:
 926        """Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.
 927
 928        Args:
 929            ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list.
 930            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent
 931                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
 932            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
 933                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
 934            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
 935                default is used.
 936            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
 937            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
 938                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
 939
 940        Returns:
 941            List of
 942            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
 943            objects.
 944        """
 945        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 946        if ids is not None:
 947            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 948        if name_contains is not None:
 949            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 950        if page is not None:
 951            params["page"] = page
 952        if page_size is not None:
 953            params["page_size"] = page_size
 954        if ordering is not None:
 955            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 956        return await self._list_resource("correspondents", Correspondent, params or None)
 957
 958    async def get_correspondent(self, id: int) -> Correspondent:
 959        """Fetch a single correspondent by its ID.
 960
 961        Args:
 962            id: Numeric correspondent ID.
 963
 964        Returns:
 965            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
 966            with the given ID.
 967
 968        Raises:
 969            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent
 970                exists with that ID.
 971        """
 972        return await self._get_resource("correspondents", id, Correspondent)
 973
 974    async def create_correspondent(
 975        self,
 976        *,
 977        name: str,
 978        match: str | None = None,
 979        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 980        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 981    ) -> Correspondent:
 982        """Create a new correspondent.
 983
 984        Args:
 985            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
 986            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 987                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 988            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 989                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 990                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 991            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 992                case-insensitively.
 993
 994        Returns:
 995            The newly created
 996            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
 997        """
 998        return await self._create_resource(
 999            "correspondents",
1000            Correspondent,
1001            name=name,
1002            match=match,
1003            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1004            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1005        )
1006
1007    async def update_correspondent(
1008        self,
1009        id: int,
1010        *,
1011        name: str | None = None,
1012        match: str | None = None,
1013        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1014        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1015    ) -> Correspondent:
1016        """Partially update a correspondent (PATCH semantics).
1017
1018        Args:
1019            id: Numeric ID of the correspondent to update.
1020            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
1021            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1022                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1023            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1024                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1025                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1026            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1027                case-insensitively.
1028
1029        Returns:
1030            The updated
1031            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
1032        """
1033        return await self._update_resource(
1034            "correspondents",
1035            id,
1036            Correspondent,
1037            name=name,
1038            match=match,
1039            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1040            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1041        )
1042
1043    async def delete_correspondent(self, id: int) -> None:
1044        """Delete a correspondent.
1045
1046        Args:
1047            id: Numeric ID of the correspondent to delete.
1048
1049        Raises:
1050            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no correspondent
1051                exists with that ID.
1052        """
1053        await self._delete_resource("correspondents", id)
1054
1055    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1056    # Document Types
1057    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1058
1059    async def list_document_types(
1060        self,
1061        *,
1062        ids: list[int] | None = None,
1063        name_contains: str | None = None,
1064        page: int | None = None,
1065        page_size: int | None = None,
1066        ordering: str | None = None,
1067        descending: bool = False,
1068    ) -> list[DocumentType]:
1069        """Return document types defined in paperless-ngx.
1070
1071        Args:
1072            ids: Return only document types whose ID is in this list.
1073            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on document-type
1074                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1075            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1076                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1077            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1078                default is used.
1079            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1080            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1081                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1082
1083        Returns:
1084            List of
1085            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`
1086            objects.
1087        """
1088        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1089        if ids is not None:
1090            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
1091        if name_contains is not None:
1092            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1093        if page is not None:
1094            params["page"] = page
1095        if page_size is not None:
1096            params["page_size"] = page_size
1097        if ordering is not None:
1098            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1099        return await self._list_resource("document_types", DocumentType, params or None)
1100
1101    async def get_document_type(self, id: int) -> DocumentType:
1102        """Fetch a single document type by its ID.
1103
1104        Args:
1105            id: Numeric document-type ID.
1106
1107        Returns:
1108            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`
1109            with the given ID.
1110
1111        Raises:
1112            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document type
1113                exists with that ID.
1114        """
1115        return await self._get_resource("document_types", id, DocumentType)
1116
1117    async def create_document_type(
1118        self,
1119        *,
1120        name: str,
1121        match: str | None = None,
1122        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1123        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1124    ) -> DocumentType:
1125        """Create a new document type.
1126
1127        Args:
1128            name: Document-type name (e.g. ``"Invoice"``, ``"Receipt"``).
1129                Must be unique.
1130            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1131                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1132            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1133                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1134                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1135            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1136                case-insensitively.
1137
1138        Returns:
1139            The newly created
1140            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`.
1141        """
1142        return await self._create_resource(
1143            "document_types",
1144            DocumentType,
1145            name=name,
1146            match=match,
1147            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1148            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1149        )
1150
1151    async def update_document_type(
1152        self,
1153        id: int,
1154        *,
1155        name: str | None = None,
1156        match: str | None = None,
1157        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1158        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1159    ) -> DocumentType:
1160        """Partially update a document type (PATCH semantics).
1161
1162        Args:
1163            id: Numeric ID of the document type to update.
1164            name: Document-type name (e.g. ``"Invoice"``, ``"Receipt"``).
1165                Must be unique.
1166            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1167                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1168            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1169                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1170                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1171            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1172                case-insensitively.
1173
1174        Returns:
1175            The updated
1176            :class:`~easypaperless.models.document_types.DocumentType`.
1177        """
1178        return await self._update_resource(
1179            "document_types",
1180            id,
1181            DocumentType,
1182            name=name,
1183            match=match,
1184            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1185            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1186        )
1187
1188    async def delete_document_type(self, id: int) -> None:
1189        """Delete a document type.
1190
1191        Args:
1192            id: Numeric ID of the document type to delete.
1193
1194        Raises:
1195            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document type
1196                exists with that ID.
1197        """
1198        await self._delete_resource("document_types", id)
1199
1200    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1201    # Storage Paths
1202    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1203
1204    async def list_storage_paths(
1205        self,
1206        *,
1207        ids: list[int] | None = None,
1208        name_contains: str | None = None,
1209        page: int | None = None,
1210        page_size: int | None = None,
1211        ordering: str | None = None,
1212        descending: bool = False,
1213    ) -> list[StoragePath]:
1214        """Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.
1215
1216        Args:
1217            ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list.
1218            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on storage-path
1219                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1220            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1221                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1222            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1223                default is used.
1224            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1225            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1226                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1227
1228        Returns:
1229            List of
1230            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1231            objects.
1232        """
1233        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1234        if ids is not None:
1235            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
1236        if name_contains is not None:
1237            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1238        if page is not None:
1239            params["page"] = page
1240        if page_size is not None:
1241            params["page_size"] = page_size
1242        if ordering is not None:
1243            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1244        return await self._list_resource("storage_paths", StoragePath, params or None)
1245
1246    async def get_storage_path(self, id: int) -> StoragePath:
1247        """Fetch a single storage path by its ID.
1248
1249        Args:
1250            id: Numeric storage-path ID.
1251
1252        Returns:
1253            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1254            with the given ID.
1255
1256        Raises:
1257            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1258                exists with that ID.
1259        """
1260        return await self._get_resource("storage_paths", id, StoragePath)
1261
1262    async def create_storage_path(
1263        self,
1264        *,
1265        name: str,
1266        path: str | None = None,
1267        match: str | None = None,
1268        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1269        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1270    ) -> StoragePath:
1271        """Create a new storage path.
1272
1273        Args:
1274            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1275            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1276                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1277                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1278                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example:
1279                ``"{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}"``. When omitted,
1280                the server default location is used.
1281            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1282                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1283            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1284                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1285                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1286            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1287                case-insensitively.
1288
1289        Returns:
1290            The newly created
1291            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1292        """
1293        return await self._create_resource(
1294            "storage_paths",
1295            StoragePath,
1296            name=name,
1297            path=path,
1298            match=match,
1299            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1300            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1301        )
1302
1303    async def update_storage_path(
1304        self,
1305        id: int,
1306        *,
1307        name: str | None = None,
1308        path: str | None = None,
1309        match: str | None = None,
1310        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1311        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1312    ) -> StoragePath:
1313        """Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).
1314
1315        Args:
1316            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update.
1317            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1318            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1319                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1320                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1321                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example: ``"{title}"``.
1322            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1323                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1324            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1325                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1326                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1327            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1328                case-insensitively.
1329
1330        Returns:
1331            The updated
1332            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1333        """
1334        return await self._update_resource(
1335            "storage_paths",
1336            id,
1337            StoragePath,
1338            name=name,
1339            path=path,
1340            match=match,
1341            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1342            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1343        )
1344
1345    async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None:
1346        """Delete a storage path.
1347
1348        Args:
1349            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.
1350
1351        Raises:
1352            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1353                exists with that ID.
1354        """
1355        await self._delete_resource("storage_paths", id)
1356
1357    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1358    # Custom Fields
1359    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1360
1361    async def list_custom_fields(
1362        self,
1363        *,
1364        page: int | None = None,
1365        page_size: int | None = None,
1366        ordering: str | None = None,
1367        descending: bool = False,
1368    ) -> list[CustomField]:
1369        """Return all custom fields defined in paperless-ngx.
1370
1371        Args:
1372            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1373                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1374            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1375                default is used.
1376            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1377            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1378                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1379
1380        Returns:
1381            List of
1382            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1383            objects.
1384        """
1385        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1386        if page is not None:
1387            params["page"] = page
1388        if page_size is not None:
1389            params["page_size"] = page_size
1390        if ordering is not None:
1391            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1392        return await self._list_resource("custom_fields", CustomField, params or None)
1393
1394    async def get_custom_field(self, id: int) -> CustomField:
1395        """Fetch a single custom field by its ID.
1396
1397        Args:
1398            id: Numeric custom-field ID.
1399
1400        Returns:
1401            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1402            with the given ID.
1403
1404        Raises:
1405            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1406                exists with that ID.
1407        """
1408        return await self._get_resource("custom_fields", id, CustomField)
1409
1410    async def create_custom_field(
1411        self,
1412        *,
1413        name: str,
1414        data_type: str,
1415        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1416    ) -> CustomField:
1417        """Create a new custom field.
1418
1419        Args:
1420            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1421            data_type: Value type. One of ``"string"``, ``"boolean"``,
1422                ``"integer"``, ``"float"``, ``"monetary"``, ``"date"``,
1423                ``"url"``, ``"documentlink"``, ``"select"``.
1424            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For
1425                ``data_type="select"``, pass
1426                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}``. For all
1427                other types, leave as ``None``.
1428
1429        Returns:
1430            The newly created
1431            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1432        """
1433        return await self._create_resource(
1434            "custom_fields",
1435            CustomField,
1436            name=name,
1437            data_type=data_type,
1438            extra_data=extra_data,
1439        )
1440
1441    async def update_custom_field(
1442        self,
1443        id: int,
1444        *,
1445        name: str | None = None,
1446        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1447    ) -> CustomField:
1448        """Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).
1449
1450        Note:
1451            ``data_type`` is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API
1452            does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To
1453            change the type, delete and recreate the field.
1454
1455        Args:
1456            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update.
1457            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1458            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g.
1459                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}`` for select
1460                fields). Passing ``None`` is a no-op and will not clear the
1461                existing value.
1462
1463        Returns:
1464            The updated
1465            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1466        """
1467        return await self._update_resource(
1468            "custom_fields",
1469            id,
1470            CustomField,
1471            name=name,
1472            extra_data=extra_data,
1473        )
1474
1475    async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None:
1476        """Delete a custom field.
1477
1478        Args:
1479            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.
1480
1481        Raises:
1482            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1483                exists with that ID.
1484        """
1485        await self._delete_resource("custom_fields", id)

Async client for the paperless-ngx API.

All operations are flat methods on the client. String names are resolved to integer IDs automatically wherever the API requires IDs (tags, correspondents, document types, storage paths).

Use as an async context manager to ensure the underlying HTTP connection pool is closed when you are done:

Example: async with PaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client: docs = await client.list_documents()

PaperlessClient( url: str, api_key: str, *, poll_interval: float = 2.0, poll_timeout: float = 60.0) 
55    def __init__(
56        self,
57        url: str,
58        api_key: str,
59        *,
60        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
61        poll_timeout: float = 60.0,
62    ) -> None:
63        """Create an async paperless-ngx client.
64
65        Args:
66            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
67                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
68            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
69                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
70            poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when ``wait=True``
71                is passed to :meth:`upload_document`.  Default: ``2.0``.
72            poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish
73                processing before raising
74                :exc:`~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError`.
75                Default: ``60.0``.
76        """
77        self._session = HttpSession(base_url=url, api_key=api_key)
78        self._resolver = NameResolver(self._session)
79        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
80        self._poll_timeout = poll_timeout

Create an async paperless-ngx client.

Args: url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance (e.g. "http://localhost:8000"). api_key: API token. Generate one in paperless-ngx under *Settings → API → Generate Token*. poll_interval: Seconds between status checks when wait=True is passed to upload_document(). Default: 2.0. poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a document to finish processing before raising ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError. Default: 60.0.

async def close(self) -> None: 
82    async def close(self) -> None:
83        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.
84
85        Called automatically when used as an async context manager.
86        """
87        await self._session.close()

Close the underlying HTTP connection pool.

Called automatically when used as an async context manager.

async def get_document( self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document: 
 99    async def get_document(self, id: int, *, include_metadata: bool = False) -> Document:
100        """Fetch a single document by its ID.
101
102        Args:
103            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
104            include_metadata: When ``True``, the extended file-level metadata
105                (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from
106                ``/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and attached to
107                :attr:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata`.
108                Default: ``False``.
109
110        Returns:
111            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document` with the
112            given ID.  When ``include_metadata=True`` the ``metadata``
113            attribute is populated; otherwise it is ``None``.
114
115        Raises:
116            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
117                with that ID.
118        """
119        if include_metadata:
120            doc_resp, meta_resp = await asyncio.gather(
121                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/"),
122                self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/"),
123            )
124            data = doc_resp.json()
125            data["metadata"] = meta_resp.json()
126        else:
127            resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/")
128            data = resp.json()
129        return Document.model_validate(data)

Fetch a single document by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID. include_metadata: When True, the extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type) is fetched concurrently from /documents/{id}/metadata/ and attached to ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.metadata. Default: False.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document with the given ID. When include_metadata=True the metadata attribute is populated; otherwise it is None.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata: 
131    async def get_document_metadata(self, id: int) -> DocumentMetadata:
132        """Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.
133
134        This is a lower-overhead alternative to
135        ``get_document(id, include_metadata=True)`` when you only need the
136        metadata and not the full document object.
137
138        Args:
139            id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.
140
141        Returns:
142            A :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata`
143            instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded
144            file metadata.
145
146        Raises:
147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
148                with that ID.
149        """
150        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{id}/metadata/")
151        return DocumentMetadata.model_validate(resp.json())

Fetch the extended file-level metadata for a document.

This is a lower-overhead alternative to get_document(id, include_metadata=True) when you only need the metadata and not the full document object.

Args: id: Numeric paperless-ngx document ID.

Returns: A ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentMetadata instance containing checksums, sizes, MIME type, and embedded file metadata.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def list_documents( self, *, search: str | None = None, search_mode: str = 'title_or_text', tags: list[int | str] | None = None, any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, created_after: str | None = None, created_before: str | None = None, added_after: str | None = None, added_before: str | None = None, modified_after: str | None = None, modified_before: str | None = None, checksum: str | None = None, page_size: int = 25, page: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False, max_results: int | None = None, on_page: Optional[Callable[[int, int | None], NoneType]] = None) -> list[Document]: 
153    async def list_documents(
154        self,
155        *,
156        search: str | None = None,
157        search_mode: str = "title_or_text",
158        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
159        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
160        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
161        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
162        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
163        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
164        document_type: int | str | None = None,
165        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
166        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
167        asn: int | None = None,
168        created_after: str | None = None,
169        created_before: str | None = None,
170        added_after: str | None = None,
171        added_before: str | None = None,
172        modified_after: str | None = None,
173        modified_before: str | None = None,
174        checksum: str | None = None,
175        page_size: int = 25,
176        page: int | None = None,
177        ordering: str | None = None,
178        descending: bool = False,
179        max_results: int | None = None,
180        on_page: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None,
181    ) -> list[Document]:
182        """Return a filtered list of documents.
183
184        All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either
185        integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.
186
187        Args:
188            search: Search string.  Behaviour depends on ``search_mode``.
189            search_mode: How ``search`` is applied.  One of:
190
191                * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
192                  title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
193                  parameter).
194                * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
195                  only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
196                * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
197                  ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
198                * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
199                  on the original file name (raw API
200                  ``original_filename__icontains``).
201
202            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
203                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
204            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
205                (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
206            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
207                Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
208            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
209                correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
210            any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
211                these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
212                Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
213            exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
214                these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
215            document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
216                Accepts a document-type ID or name.
217            any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
218                these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
219                precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
220            exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
221                these.  Accepts IDs or names.
222            asn: Filter by archive serial number.
223            created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
224                documents created **after** this date are returned.
225            created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
226                documents created **before** this date are returned.
227            added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
228                documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
229            added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
230                documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
231            modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
232                documents **modified** after this date are returned.
233            modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
234                documents **modified** before this date are returned.
235            checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
236                Returns only the document whose original file matches this
237                checksum.
238            page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
239                Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
240                result sets.
241            page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
242                set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
243                that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
244                to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
245                all pages).
246            ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
247                ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
248                control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
249            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
250                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
251                Default: ``False``.
252            max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
253                been collected and return at most this many results.
254                ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.
255            on_page: Optional callback invoked after each API page is fetched.
256                Receives ``(fetched_so_far, total)`` where ``total`` is the
257                server-reported document count from the first page response
258                (``None`` if not reported).  Ignored when ``page`` is set.
259
260        Returns:
261            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
262            objects.
263        """
264        params: dict[str, Any] = {"page_size": page_size}
265
266        if search is not None:
267            api_param = _SEARCH_MODE_MAP.get(search_mode, "search")
268            params[api_param] = search
269
270        if tags is not None:
271            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
272            params["tags__id__all"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
273
274        if any_tag is not None:
275            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", any_tag)
276            params["tags__id__in"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
277
278        if exclude_tags is not None:
279            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", exclude_tags)
280            params["tags__id__none"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
281
282        if any_correspondent is not None:
283            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", any_correspondent)
284            params["correspondent__id__in"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
285        elif correspondent is not None:
