286            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
287            params["correspondent__id__in"] = resolved_id
288
289        if exclude_correspondents is not None:
290            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", exclude_correspondents)
291            params["correspondent__id__none"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
292
293        if any_document_type is not None:
294            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", any_document_type)
295            params["document_type__id__in"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
296        elif document_type is not None:
297            resolved_id = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
298            params["document_type"] = resolved_id
299
300        if exclude_document_types is not None:
301            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("document_types", exclude_document_types)
302            params["document_type__id__none"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
303
304        if asn is not None:
305            params["archive_serial_number"] = asn
306
307        if created_after is not None:
308            params["created__date__gt"] = created_after
309
310        if created_before is not None:
311            params["created__date__lt"] = created_before
312
313        if added_after is not None:
314            params["added__date__gt"] = added_after
315
316        if added_before is not None:
317            params["added__date__lt"] = added_before
318
319        if modified_after is not None:
320            params["modified__date__gt"] = modified_after
321
322        if modified_before is not None:
323            params["modified__date__lt"] = modified_before
324
325        if checksum is not None:
326            params["checksum"] = checksum
327
328        if ordering is not None:
329            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
330
331        if page is not None:
332            params["page"] = page
333            resp = await self._session.get("/documents/", params=params)
334            items = resp.json().get("results", [])
335            if max_results is not None:
336                items = items[:max_results]
337            return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]
338
339        items = await self._session.get_all_pages(
340            "/documents/", params, max_results=max_results, on_page=on_page
341        )
342        return [Document.model_validate(item) for item in items]

Return a filtered list of documents.

All tag, correspondent, and document-type parameters accept either integer IDs or string names — names are resolved to IDs transparently.

Args: search: Search string. Behaviour depends on search_mode. search_mode: How search is applied. One of:

    * ``"title_or_text"`` *(default)* — full-text search across
      title and OCR content (Whoosh FTS, raw API ``search``
      parameter).
    * ``"title"`` — case-insensitive substring match on title
      only (raw API ``title__icontains``).
    * ``"query"`` — paperless query language, e.g.
      ``"tag:invoice date:[2024 TO *]"`` (raw API ``query``).
    * ``"original_filename"`` — case-insensitive substring match
      on the original file name (raw API
      ``original_filename__icontains``).

tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
    Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags
    (OR semantics).  Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
    Accepts tag IDs or tag names.
correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to exactly this
    correspondent.  Accepts a correspondent ID or name.
any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to **any** of
    these correspondents (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.
    Takes precedence over ``correspondent`` when both are given.
exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of
    these correspondents.  Accepts IDs or names.
document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
    Accepts a document-type ID or name.
any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is **any** of
    these (OR semantics).  Accepts IDs or names.  Takes
    precedence over ``document_type`` when both are given.
exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of
    these.  Accepts IDs or names.
asn: Filter by archive serial number.
created_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents created **after** this date are returned.
created_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents created **before** this date are returned.
added_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **added** (ingested) after this date are returned.
added_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **added** (ingested) before this date are returned.
modified_after: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **modified** after this date are returned.
modified_before: ISO-8601 date string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).  Only
    documents **modified** before this date are returned.
checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
    Returns only the document whose original file matches this
    checksum.
page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
    Increase to reduce the number of HTTP requests for large
    result sets.
page: Return only this specific page of results (1-based). When
    set, auto-pagination is disabled and only the results from
    that single page are returned. Use together with ``page_size``
    to control page size. Default: ``None`` (auto-paginate through
    all pages).
ordering: Field name to sort by. Examples: ``"created"``,
    ``"title"``, ``"added"``. Use together with ``descending`` to
    control direction. Default: ``None`` (server default ordering).
descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
    ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
    Default: ``False``.
max_results: Stop fetching pages once this many documents have
    been collected and return at most this many results.
    ``None`` *(default)* returns everything.
on_page: Optional callback invoked after each API page is fetched.
    Receives ``(fetched_so_far, total)`` where ``total`` is the
    server-reported document count from the first page response
    (``None`` if not reported).  Ignored when ``page`` is set.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document objects.

async def update_document( self, id: int, *, title: str | None = None, date: str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, storage_path: int | str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None) -> Document: 
344    async def update_document(
345        self,
346        id: int,
347        *,
348        title: str | None = None,
349        date: str | None = None,
350        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
351        document_type: int | str | None = None,
352        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
353        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
354        asn: int | None = None,
355        custom_fields: list[dict] | None = None,
356    ) -> Document:
357        """Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).
358
359        Args:
360            id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
361            title: New document title.
362            date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
363            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
364                Pass ``0`` to clear.
365            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
366                Pass ``0`` to clear.
367            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
368                Pass ``0`` to clear.
369            tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).  The existing
370                tag list is replaced, not merged.
371            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
372            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}``
373                dicts.  Replaces the existing custom-field values.
374
375        Returns:
376            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
377        """
378        logger.debug("Updating document id=%d", id)
379        payload: dict[str, Any] = {}
380
381        if title is not None:
382            payload["title"] = title
383        if date is not None:
384            payload["created"] = date
385        if correspondent is not None:
386            payload["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
387        if document_type is not None:
388            payload["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
389        if storage_path is not None:
390            payload["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
391        if tags is not None:
392            payload["tags"] = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
393        if asn is not None:
394            payload["archive_serial_number"] = asn
395        if custom_fields is not None:
396            payload["custom_fields"] = custom_fields
397
398        resp = await self._session.patch(f"/documents/{id}/", json=payload)
399        return Document.model_validate(resp.json())

Partially update a document (PATCH semantics — only passed fields change).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to update. title: New document title. date: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name. Pass 0 to clear. tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names). The existing tag list is replaced, not merged. asn: Archive serial number to assign. custom_fields: List of {"field": <field_id>, "value": ...} dicts. Replaces the existing custom-field values.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.

async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None: 
401    async def delete_document(self, id: int) -> None:
402        """Permanently delete a document.
403
404        Args:
405            id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
406
407        Raises:
408            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
409                with that ID.
410        """
411        logger.debug("Deleting document id=%d", id)
412        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{id}/")

Permanently delete a document.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes: 
414    async def download_document(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes:
415        """Download the binary content of a document.
416
417        Args:
418            id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
419            original: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns the archived
420                (post-processed) PDF.  If ``True``, returns the original
421                file that was uploaded.
422
423        Returns:
424            Raw file bytes.
425        """
426        endpoint = "download" if original else "archive"
427        resp = await self._session.get_download(f"/documents/{id}/{endpoint}/")
428        content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "")
429        if "text/html" in content_type or resp.content[:9].lower().startswith(b"<!doctype"):
430            raise ServerError(
431                f"Download returned an HTML page (content-type: {content_type!r}). "
432                "The server redirected to a login page even after re-attaching auth. "
433                "Run with --verbose to see redirect details.",
434                status_code=None,
435            )
436        return resp.content

Download the binary content of a document.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document to download. original: If False (default), returns the archived (post-processed) PDF. If True, returns the original file that was uploaded.

Returns: Raw file bytes.

async def get_notes( self, document_id: int) -> list[DocumentNote]: 
442    async def get_notes(self, document_id: int) -> list[DocumentNote]:
443        """Fetch all notes attached to a document.
444
445        Args:
446            document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.
447
448        Returns:
449            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote` objects,
450            ordered by creation time.
451
452        Raises:
453            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
454                with that ID.
455        """
456        logger.debug("Fetching notes for document id=%d", document_id)
457        resp = await self._session.get(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/")
458        return [DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in resp.json()]

Fetch all notes attached to a document.

Args: document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote objects, ordered by creation time.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def create_note( self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> DocumentNote: 
460    async def create_note(self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> DocumentNote:
461        """Create a new note on a document.
462
463        Args:
464            document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate.
465            note: Text content of the note.
466
467        Returns:
468            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote`.
469
470        Raises:
471            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
472                with that ID.
473        """
474        logger.debug("Creating note for document id=%d", document_id)
475        resp = await self._session.post(
476            f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/",
477            json={"note": note},
478        )
479        # paperless-ngx returns the full list of notes after creation;
480        # the newly created note is the last item in the list.
481        data = resp.json()
482        if isinstance(data, list):
483            return DocumentNote.model_validate(data[-1])
484        return DocumentNote.model_validate(data)

Create a new note on a document.

Args: document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate. note: Text content of the note.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.

async def delete_note(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None: 
486    async def delete_note(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None:
487        """Delete a note from a document.
488
489        Args:
490            document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note.
491            note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.
492
493        Raises:
494            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note
495                exists with the given IDs.
496        """
497        logger.debug("Deleting note id=%d from document id=%d", note_id, document_id)
498        await self._session.delete(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/", params={"id": note_id})

Delete a note from a document.

Args: document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note. note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note exists with the given IDs.

async def upload_document( self, file: str | pathlib._local.Path, *, title: str | None = None, created: str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, storage_path: int | str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, asn: int | None = None, wait: bool = False, poll_interval: float | None = None, poll_timeout: float | None = None) -> str | Document: 
504    async def upload_document(
505        self,
506        file: str | Path,
507        *,
508        title: str | None = None,
509        created: str | None = None,
510        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
511        document_type: int | str | None = None,
512        storage_path: int | str | None = None,
513        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
514        asn: int | None = None,
515        wait: bool = False,
516        poll_interval: float | None = None,
517        poll_timeout: float | None = None,
518    ) -> str | Document:
519        """Upload a document to paperless-ngx.
520
521        Args:
522            file: Path to the file to upload (``str`` or
523                :class:`~pathlib.Path`).
524            title: Title to assign to the document.  Paperless-ngx derives
525                one from the file name if omitted.
526            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``).
527            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
528            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
529            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
530            tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
531            asn: Archive serial number to assign.
532            wait: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns immediately with the
533                task ID string.  If ``True``, polls the task-status endpoint
534                until processing completes and returns the resulting
535                :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
536            poll_interval: Override the client-level ``poll_interval`` for
537                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
538            poll_timeout: Override the client-level ``poll_timeout`` for
539                this upload.  Only used when ``wait=True``.
540
541        Returns:
542            The Celery task ID string when ``wait=False``, or the fully
543            processed :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
544            when ``wait=True``.
545
546        Raises:
547            ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports
548                that the processing task failed.
549            ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If ``wait=True`` and
550                processing does not complete within the timeout.
551        """
552        file_path = Path(file)
553        file_bytes = file_path.read_bytes()
554        logger.info("Uploading %r (%d bytes)", file_path.name, len(file_bytes))
555
556        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
557        if title is not None:
558            data["title"] = title
559        if created is not None:
560            data["created"] = created
561        if correspondent is not None:
562            data["correspondent"] = await self._resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
563        if document_type is not None:
564            data["document_type"] = await self._resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
565        if storage_path is not None:
566            data["storage_path"] = await self._resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
567        if tags is not None:
568            resolved = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
569            data["tags"] = resolved
570        if asn is not None:
571            data["archive_serial_number"] = asn
572
573        files = {"document": (file_path.name, file_bytes)}
574        resp = await self._session.post("/documents/post_document/", data=data, files=files)
575        task_id: str = resp.text.strip('"')
576        logger.debug("Upload accepted, task_id=%r", task_id)
577
578        if not wait:
579            return task_id
580
581        interval = poll_interval if poll_interval is not None else self._poll_interval
582        timeout = poll_timeout if poll_timeout is not None else self._poll_timeout
583        return await self._poll_task(task_id, poll_interval=interval, poll_timeout=timeout)

Upload a document to paperless-ngx.

Args: file: Path to the file to upload (str or ~pathlib.Path). title: Title to assign to the document. Paperless-ngx derives one from the file name if omitted. created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name. document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name. storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name. tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names. asn: Archive serial number to assign. wait: If False (default), returns immediately with the task ID string. If True, polls the task-status endpoint until processing completes and returns the resulting ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document. poll_interval: Override the client-level poll_interval for this upload. Only used when wait=True. poll_timeout: Override the client-level poll_timeout for this upload. Only used when wait=True.

Returns: The Celery task ID string when wait=False, or the fully processed ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document when wait=True.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If paperless-ngx reports that the processing task failed. ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If wait=True and processing does not complete within the timeout.

async def bulk_edit(self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any) -> None: 
617    async def bulk_edit(
618        self, document_ids: list[int], method: str, **parameters: Any
619    ) -> None:
620        """Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.
621
622        This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as
623        :meth:`bulk_add_tag`, :meth:`bulk_remove_tag`, and
624        :meth:`bulk_modify_tags`.
625
626        Args:
627            document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on.
628            method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx
629                (e.g. ``"add_tag"``, ``"remove_tag"``, ``"delete"``).
630            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as
631                the ``parameters`` object.
632        """
633        payload = {"documents": document_ids, "method": method, "parameters": parameters}
634        await self._session.post("/documents/bulk_edit/", json=payload)

Execute a bulk-edit operation on a list of documents.

This is a low-level method; prefer the higher-level helpers such as bulk_add_tag(), bulk_remove_tag(), and bulk_modify_tags().

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to operate on. method: Bulk-edit method name as recognised by paperless-ngx (e.g. "add_tag", "remove_tag", "delete"). **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the API as the parameters object.

async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None: 
636    async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
637        """Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.
638
639        Args:
640            document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
641            tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
642        """
643        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
644        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "add_tag", tag=tag_id)

Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to tag. tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.

async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None: 
646    async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: list[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
647        """Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.
648
649        Args:
650            document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag.
651            tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
652        """
653        tag_id = await self._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
654        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "remove_tag", tag=tag_id)

Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag. tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.

async def bulk_modify_tags( self, document_ids: list[int], *, add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None) -> None: 
656    async def bulk_modify_tags(
657        self,
658        document_ids: list[int],
659        *,
660        add_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
661        remove_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
662    ) -> None:
663        """Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.
664
665        Args:
666            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
667            add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
668            remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.
669        """
670        add_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", add_tags or [])
671        remove_ids = await self._resolver.resolve_list("tags", remove_tags or [])
672        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "modify_tags", add_tags=add_ids, remove_tags=remove_ids)

Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to modify. add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names. remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.

async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None: 
674    async def bulk_delete(self, document_ids: list[int]) -> None:
675        """Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.
676
677        Args:
678            document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.
679        """
680        await self.bulk_edit(document_ids, "delete")

Permanently delete multiple documents in a single request.

Args: document_ids: List of document IDs to delete.

async def bulk_edit_objects( self, object_type: str, object_ids: list[int], operation: str, **parameters: Any) -> None: 
682    async def bulk_edit_objects(
683        self,
684        object_type: str,
685        object_ids: list[int],
686        operation: str,
687        **parameters: Any,
688    ) -> None:
689        """Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).
690
691        Args:
692            object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g.
693                ``"tags"``, ``"correspondents"``).
694            object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on.
695            operation: Operation name recognised by the
696                ``/bulk_edit_objects/`` endpoint.
697            **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to
698                the API payload.
699        """
700        payload = {
701            "objects": object_ids,
702            "object_type": object_type,
703            "operation": operation,
704            **parameters,
705        }
706        await self._session.post("/bulk_edit_objects/", json=payload)

Execute a bulk operation on non-document objects (tags, etc.).

Args: object_type: The paperless-ngx object type string (e.g. "tags", "correspondents"). object_ids: List of object IDs to operate on. operation: Operation name recognised by the /bulk_edit_objects/ endpoint. **parameters: Additional keyword arguments forwarded directly to the API payload.

async def list_tags( self, *, ids: list[int] | None = None, name_contains: str | None = None, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[Tag]: 
752    async def list_tags(
753        self,
754        *,
755        ids: list[int] | None = None,
756        name_contains: str | None = None,
757        page: int | None = None,
758        page_size: int | None = None,
759        ordering: str | None = None,
760        descending: bool = False,
761    ) -> list[Tag]:
762        """Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.
763
764        Args:
765            ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
766            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name
767                (raw API ``name__icontains``).
768            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
769                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
770            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
771                default is used.
772            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
773            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
774                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
775
776        Returns:
777            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` objects.
778        """
779        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
780        if ids is not None:
781            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
782        if name_contains is not None:
783            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
784        if page is not None:
785            params["page"] = page
786        if page_size is not None:
787            params["page_size"] = page_size
788        if ordering is not None:
789            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
790        return await self._list_resource("tags", Tag, params or None)

Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list. name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name (raw API name__icontains). page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables auto-pagination. Default: None (return all). page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag objects.

async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag: 
792    async def get_tag(self, id: int) -> Tag:
793        """Fetch a single tag by its ID.
794
795        Args:
796            id: Numeric tag ID.
797
798        Returns:
799            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` with the given ID.
800
801        Raises:
802            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
803                that ID.
804        """
805        return await self._get_resource("tags", id, Tag)

Fetch a single tag by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric tag ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with that ID.

async def create_tag( self, *, name: str, color: str | None = None, is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None, parent: int | None = None) -> Tag: 
807    async def create_tag(
808        self,
809        *,
810        name: str,
811        color: str | None = None,
812        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
813        match: str | None = None,
814        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
815        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
816        parent: int | None = None,
817    ) -> Tag:
818        """Create a new tag.
819
820        Args:
821            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
822            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
823                string (e.g. ``"#ff0000"``).
824            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
825                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
826                the inbox tag.
827            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
828                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
829            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
830                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
831                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
832            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
833                case-insensitively.
834            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
835                hierarchical tag trees. ``None`` creates a root-level tag.
836
837        Returns:
838            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
839        """
840        return await self._create_resource(
841            "tags",
842            Tag,
843            name=name,
844            color=color,
845            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
846            match=match,
847            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
848            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
849            parent=parent,
850        )

Create a new tag.

Args: name: Tag name. Must be unique. color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex string (e.g. "#ff0000"). is_inbox_tag: When True, newly ingested documents receive this tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be the inbox tag. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively. parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling hierarchical tag trees. None creates a root-level tag.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag.

async def update_tag( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, color: str | None = None, is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None, parent: int | None = None) -> Tag: 
852    async def update_tag(
853        self,
854        id: int,
855        *,
856        name: str | None = None,
857        color: str | None = None,
858        is_inbox_tag: bool | None = None,
859        match: str | None = None,
860        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
861        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
862        parent: int | None = None,
863    ) -> Tag:
864        """Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).
865
866        Args:
867            id: Numeric ID of the tag to update.
868            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
869            color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex
870                string (e.g. ``"#00ff00"``).
871            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents receive this
872                tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be
873                the inbox tag.
874            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
875                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
876            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
877                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
878                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
879            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
880                case-insensitively.
881            parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling
882                hierarchical tag trees.
883
884        Returns:
885            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
886        """
887        return await self._update_resource(
888            "tags",
889            id,
890            Tag,
891            name=name,
892            color=color,
893            is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
894            match=match,
895            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
896            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
897            parent=parent,
898        )

Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the tag to update. name: Tag name. Must be unique. color: Background colour in the paperless-ngx UI as a CSS hex string (e.g. "#00ff00"). is_inbox_tag: When True, newly ingested documents receive this tag automatically until processed. At most one tag should be the inbox tag. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively. parent: ID of an existing tag to use as parent, enabling hierarchical tag trees.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag.

async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None: 
900    async def delete_tag(self, id: int) -> None:
901        """Delete a tag.
902
903        Args:
904            id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.
905
906        Raises:
907            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
908                that ID.
909        """
910        await self._delete_resource("tags", id)

Delete a tag.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with that ID.

async def list_correspondents( self, *, ids: list[int] | None = None, name_contains: str | None = None, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[Correspondent]: 
916    async def list_correspondents(
917        self,
918        *,
919        ids: list[int] | None = None,
920        name_contains: str | None = None,
921        page: int | None = None,
922        page_size: int | None = None,
923        ordering: str | None = None,
924        descending: bool = False,
925    ) -> list[Correspondent]:
926        """Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.
927
928        Args:
929            ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list.
930            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent
931                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
932            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
933                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
934            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
935                default is used.
936            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
937            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
938                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
939
940        Returns:
941            List of
942            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`
943            objects.
944        """
945        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
946        if ids is not None:
947            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
948        if name_contains is not None:
949            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
950        if page is not None:
951            params["page"] = page
952        if page_size is not None:
953            params["page_size"] = page_size
954        if ordering is not None:
955            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
956        return await self._list_resource("correspondents", Correspondent, params or None)

Return correspondents defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: ids: Return only correspondents whose ID is in this list. name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on correspondent name (raw API name__icontains). page: Return only this specific page (1-based). async def create_correspondent( self, *, name: str, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> Correspondent: 
 974    async def create_correspondent(
 975        self,
 976        *,
 977        name: str,
 978        match: str | None = None,
 979        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
 980        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
 981    ) -> Correspondent:
 982        """Create a new correspondent.
 983
 984        Args:
 985            name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique.
 986            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
 987                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
 988            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
 989                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
 990                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
 991            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
 992                case-insensitively.
 993
 994        Returns:
 995            The newly created
 996            :class:`~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent`.
 997        """
 998        return await self._create_resource(
 999            "correspondents",
1000            Correspondent,
1001            name=name,
1002            match=match,
1003            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1004            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1005        )

Create a new correspondent.

Args: name: Correspondent name (sender/recipient). Must be unique. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.correspondents.Correspondent.

1097        if ordering is not None:

Delete a document type.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the document type to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document type exists with that ID.

async def list_storage_paths( self, *, ids: list[int] | None = None, name_contains: str | None = None, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[StoragePath]: 
1204    async def list_storage_paths(
1205        self,
1206        *,
1207        ids: list[int] | None = None,
1208        name_contains: str | None = None,
1209        page: int | None = None,
1210        page_size: int | None = None,
1211        ordering: str | None = None,
1212        descending: bool = False,
1213    ) -> list[StoragePath]:
1214        """Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.
1215
1216        Args:
1217            ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list.
1218            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on storage-path
1219                name (raw API ``name__icontains``).
1220            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1221                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1222            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1223                default is used.
1224            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1225            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1226                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1227
1228        Returns:
1229            List of
1230            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1231            objects.
1232        """
1233        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1234        if ids is not None:
1235            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
1236        if name_contains is not None:
1237            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
1238        if page is not None:
1239            params["page"] = page
1240        if page_size is not None:
1241            params["page_size"] = page_size
1242        if ordering is not None:
1243            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1244        return await self._list_resource("storage_paths", StoragePath, params or None)

Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.

Args: ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list. name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on storage-path name (raw API name__icontains). page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables auto-pagination. Default: None (return all). page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath objects.

async def get_storage_path(self, id: int) -> StoragePath: 
1246    async def get_storage_path(self, id: int) -> StoragePath:
1247        """Fetch a single storage path by its ID.
1248
1249        Args:
1250            id: Numeric storage-path ID.
1251
1252        Returns:
1253            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`
1254            with the given ID.
1255
1256        Raises:
1257            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1258                exists with that ID.
1259        """
1260        return await self._get_resource("storage_paths", id, StoragePath)

Fetch a single storage path by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric storage-path ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path exists with that ID.

async def create_storage_path( self, *, name: str, path: str | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> StoragePath: 
1262    async def create_storage_path(
1263        self,
1264        *,
1265        name: str,
1266        path: str | None = None,
1267        match: str | None = None,
1268        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1269        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1270    ) -> StoragePath:
1271        """Create a new storage path.
1272
1273        Args:
1274            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1275            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1276                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1277                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1278                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example:
1279                ``"{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}"``. When omitted,
1280                the server default location is used.
1281            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1282                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1283            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1284                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1285                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1286            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1287                case-insensitively.
1288
1289        Returns:
1290            The newly created
1291            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1292        """
1293        return await self._create_resource(
1294            "storage_paths",
1295            StoragePath,
1296            name=name,
1297            path=path,
1298            match=match,
1299            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1300            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1301        )

Create a new storage path.

Args: name: Storage-path name. Must be unique. path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported placeholders: {created_year}, {created_month}, {created_day}, {correspondent}, {document_type}, {title}, {asn}. Example: "{created_year}/{correspondent}/{title}". When omitted, the server default location is used. match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath.

async def update_storage_path( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, path: str | None = None, match: str | None = None, matching_algorithm: int | None = None, is_insensitive: bool | None = None) -> StoragePath: 
1303    async def update_storage_path(
1304        self,
1305        id: int,
1306        *,
1307        name: str | None = None,
1308        path: str | None = None,
1309        match: str | None = None,
1310        matching_algorithm: int | None = None,
1311        is_insensitive: bool | None = None,
1312    ) -> StoragePath:
1313        """Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).
1314
1315        Args:
1316            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update.
1317            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
1318            path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported
1319                placeholders: ``{created_year}``, ``{created_month}``,
1320                ``{created_day}``, ``{correspondent}``, ``{document_type}``,
1321                ``{title}``, ``{asn}``. Example: ``"{title}"``.
1322            match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document
1323                content. Interpretation depends on ``matching_algorithm``.
1324            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied: ``0``=none,
1325                ``1``=any word, ``2``=all words, ``3``=exact, ``4``=regex,
1326                ``5``=fuzzy, ``6``=auto (ML).
1327            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is evaluated
1328                case-insensitively.
1329
1330        Returns:
1331            The updated
1332            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
1333        """
1334        return await self._update_resource(
1335            "storage_paths",
1336            id,
1337            StoragePath,
1338            name=name,
1339            path=path,
1340            match=match,
1341            matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
1342            is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
1343        )

Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).

Args: id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update. name: Storage-path name. Must be unique. path: Template string for the archive file path. Supported placeholders: {created_year}, {created_month}, {created_day}, {correspondent}, {document_type}, {title}, {asn}. Example: "{title}". match: Auto-matching pattern tested against incoming document content. Interpretation depends on matching_algorithm. matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied: 0=none, 1=any word, 2=all words, 3=exact, 4=regex, 5=fuzzy, 6=auto (ML). is_insensitive: When True, match is evaluated case-insensitively.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath.

async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None: 
1345    async def delete_storage_path(self, id: int) -> None:
1346        """Delete a storage path.
1347
1348        Args:
1349            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.
1350
1351        Raises:
1352            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path
1353                exists with that ID.
1354        """
1355        await self._delete_resource("storage_paths", id)

Delete a storage path.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path exists with that ID.

async def list_custom_fields( self, *, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False) -> list[CustomField]: 
1361    async def list_custom_fields(
1362        self,
1363        *,
1364        page: int | None = None,
1365        page_size: int | None = None,
1366        ordering: str | None = None,
1367        descending: bool = False,
1368    ) -> list[CustomField]:

1370
1371        Args:
1372            page: Return only this specific page (1-based). Disables
1373                auto-pagination. Default: ``None`` (return all).
1374            page_size: Number of results per page. When omitted, the server
1375                default is used.
1376            ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: ``"name"``, ``"id"``.
1377            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction of
1378                ``ordering``. Ignored when ``ordering`` is ``None``.
1379
1380        Returns:
1381            List of
1382            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
1383            objects.
1384        """
1385        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
1386        if page is not None:
1387            params["page"] = page
1388        if page_size is not None:
1389            params["page_size"] = page_size
1390        if ordering is not None:
1391            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
1392        return await self._list_resource("custom_fields", CustomField, params or None)

When omitted, the server default is used. ordering: Field to sort by. Examples: "name", "id". descending: When True, reverses the sort direction of ordering. Ignored when ordering is None.

1395        """Fetch a single custom field by its ID.

1398            id: Numeric custom-field ID.

1400        Returns:

Fetch a single custom field by its ID.

Args: id: Numeric custom-field ID.

Returns: The ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField with the given ID.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field exists with that ID.

async def create_custom_field( self, *, name: str, data_type: str, extra_data: typing.Any | None = None) -> CustomField: 
1410    async def create_custom_field(
1411        self,
1412        *,
1413        name: str,
1414        data_type: str,
1415        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1416    ) -> CustomField:
1417        """Create a new custom field.
1418
1419        Args:
1420            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1421            data_type: Value type. One of ``"string"``, ``"boolean"``,
1422                ``"integer"``, ``"float"``, ``"monetary"``, ``"date"``,
1423                ``"url"``, ``"documentlink"``, ``"select"``.
1424            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For
1425                ``data_type="select"``, pass
1426                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}``. For all
1427                other types, leave as ``None``.
1428
1429        Returns:
1430            The newly created
1431            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1432        """
1433        return await self._create_resource(
1434            "custom_fields",
1435            CustomField,
1436            name=name,
1437            data_type=data_type,
1438            extra_data=extra_data,
1439        )

Create a new custom field.

Args: name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique. data_type: Value type. One of "string", "boolean", "integer", "float", "monetary", "date", "url", "documentlink", "select". extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type. For data_type="select", pass {"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}. For all other types, leave as None.

Returns: The newly created ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField.

async def update_custom_field( self, id: int, *, name: str | None = None, extra_data: typing.Any | None = None) -> CustomField: 
1441    async def update_custom_field(
1442        self,
1443        id: int,
1444        *,
1445        name: str | None = None,
1446        extra_data: Any | None = None,
1447    ) -> CustomField:
1448        """Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).
1449
1450        Note:
1451            ``data_type`` is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API
1452            does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To
1453            change the type, delete and recreate the field.
1454
1455        Args:
1456            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update.
1457            name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
1458            extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g.
1459                ``{"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]}`` for select
1460                fields). Passing ``None`` is a no-op and will not clear the
1461                existing value.
1462
1463        Returns:
1464            The updated
1465            :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`.
1466        """
1467        return await self._update_resource(
1468            "custom_fields",
1469            id,
1470            CustomField,
1471            name=name,
1472            extra_data=extra_data,
1473        )

Partially update a custom field (PATCH semantics).

Note: data_type is intentionally excluded — the paperless-ngx API does not allow changing the type of an existing custom field. To change the type, delete and recreate the field.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the custom field to update. name: Field name shown in the UI. Must be unique. extra_data: Additional configuration for the field type (e.g. {"select_options": ["Option A", "Option B"]} for select fields). Passing None is a no-op and will not clear the existing value.

Returns: The updated ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField.

async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None: 
1475    async def delete_custom_field(self, id: int) -> None:
1476        """Delete a custom field.
1477
1478        Args:
1479            id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.
1480
1481        Raises:
1482            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field
1483                exists with that ID.
1484        """
1485        await self._delete_resource("custom_fields", id)

Delete a custom field.

Args: id: Numeric ID of the custom field to delete.

Raises: ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no custom field exists with that ID.

class SyncPaperlessClient: 
18class SyncPaperlessClient:
19    """Synchronous interface to paperless-ngx.
20
21    Exposes the same methods as
22    :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` but runs them
23    synchronously, making it suitable for scripts and REPL sessions that do
24    not use ``asyncio``.
25
26    All async methods of ``PaperlessClient`` are available here with the same
27    signatures.  Operations run on a dedicated background event loop thread so
28    that the httpx connection pool is reused across calls and cleanup works
29    correctly — unlike ``asyncio.run()``, which creates and destroys a loop
30    per call and leaves transports in a broken state on subsequent calls.
31
32    Use as a context manager to ensure proper cleanup:
33
34    Example:
35        with SyncPaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client:
36            tags = client.list_tags()
37            docs = client.list_documents(search="invoice")
38
39    Note:
40        Cannot be used inside an already-running event loop (e.g. Jupyter
41        notebooks).  Use :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient`
42        directly in those environments.
43
44    No business logic lives here — this class is a pure thin wrapper.
45    """
46
47    def __init__(self, url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
48        """Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.
49
50        Args:
51            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
52                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
53            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
54                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
55            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to
56                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` (e.g.
57                ``poll_interval``, ``poll_timeout``).
58        """
59        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
60        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._loop.run_forever, daemon=True)
61        self._thread.start()
62        self._async_client = PaperlessClient(url, api_key, **kwargs)
63
64    def _run(self, coro: Any) -> Any:
65        """Submit a coroutine to the background event loop and block until done."""
66        future = asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(coro, self._loop)
67        return future.result()
68
69    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
70        attr = getattr(self._async_client, name)
71        if inspect.iscoroutinefunction(attr):
72            def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
73                return self._run(attr(*args, **kwargs))
74            wrapper.__name__ = name
75            return wrapper
76        return attr
77
78    def close(self) -> None:
79        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.
80
81        Called automatically when used as a context manager.
82        """
83        self._run(self._async_client.close())
84        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._loop.stop)
85        self._thread.join()
86        self._loop.close()
87
88    def __enter__(self) -> SyncPaperlessClient:
89        return self
90
91    def __exit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
92        self.close()

Synchronous interface to paperless-ngx.

Exposes the same methods as ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient but runs them synchronously, making it suitable for scripts and REPL sessions that do not use asyncio.

All async methods of PaperlessClient are available here with the same signatures. Operations run on a dedicated background event loop thread so that the httpx connection pool is reused across calls and cleanup works correctly — unlike asyncio.run(), which creates and destroys a loop per call and leaves transports in a broken state on subsequent calls.

Use as a context manager to ensure proper cleanup:

Example: with SyncPaperlessClient(url="http://localhost:8000", api_key="abc") as client: tags = client.list_tags() docs = client.list_documents(search="invoice")

Note: Cannot be used inside an already-running event loop (e.g. Jupyter notebooks). Use ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient directly in those environments.

No business logic lives here — this class is a pure thin wrapper.

SyncPaperlessClient(url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) 
47    def __init__(self, url: str, api_key: str, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
48        """Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.
49
50        Args:
51            url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance
52                (e.g. ``"http://localhost:8000"``).
53            api_key: API token.  Generate one in paperless-ngx under
54                *Settings → API → Generate Token*.
55            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to
56                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` (e.g.
57                ``poll_interval``, ``poll_timeout``).
58        """
59        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
60        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._loop.run_forever, daemon=True)
61        self._thread.start()
62        self._async_client = PaperlessClient(url, api_key, **kwargs)

Create a synchronous paperless-ngx client.

Args: url: Base URL of the paperless-ngx instance (e.g. "http://localhost:8000"). api_key: API token. Generate one in paperless-ngx under Settings → API → Generate Token. **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments forwarded to ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient (e.g. poll_interval, poll_timeout).

def close(self) -> None: 
78    def close(self) -> None:
79        """Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.
80
81        Called automatically when used as a context manager.
82        """
83        self._run(self._async_client.close())
84        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._loop.stop)
85        self._thread.join()
86        self._loop.close()

Close the underlying HTTP connection pool and stop the event loop.

Called automatically when used as a context manager.

class DocumentStore: 
139class DocumentStore:
140    """SQLite-backed local mirror of paperless-ngx document metadata.
141
142    Wraps a :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` and mirrors
143    document metadata (plus tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
144    paths) into a local SQLite database for fast, offline-capable search.
145
146    Sync is **manual and explicit**: call :meth:`sync` to pull fresh data from
147    the server.  :meth:`search_documents` is a pure SQLite query — no network
148    is involved.
149
150    Example:
151        async with PaperlessClient(url="...", api_key="...") as client:
152            store = DocumentStore(client, "paperless.db")
153            await store.sync()
154            results = store.search_documents(tags=["invoice"], created_after="2024-01-01")
155            store.close()
156    """
157
158    def __init__(self, client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
159        """Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.
160
161        Args:
162            client: An initialised
163                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` used for
164                fetching data during :meth:`sync`.
165            db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.  The file is created
166                if it does not exist.
167        """
168        self._client = client
169        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
170        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None
171
172    def _get_conn(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
173        if self._conn is None:
174            logger.debug("Opening SQLite database at %s", self._db_path)
175            self._conn = sqlite3.connect(self._db_path)
176            self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
177            self._conn.executescript(_DDL)
178            self._conn.commit()
179        return self._conn
180
181    def close(self) -> None:
182        """Close the SQLite database connection."""
183        if self._conn is not None:
184            self._conn.close()
185            self._conn = None
186
187    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
188    # Sync
189    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
190
191    async def sync(
192        self,
193        *,
194        force_full: bool = False,
195        on_doc_fetched: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None,
196    ) -> int:
197        """Pull documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.
198
199        On the first call (no ``last_sync`` recorded) a full fetch is performed.
200        On subsequent calls only documents modified after the previous sync date
201        are fetched (incremental sync), which is much faster for large archives.
202
203        Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
204        paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite.  Existing
205        local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server
206        are *not* removed by incremental syncs.
207
208        Args:
209            force_full: If ``True``, ignore ``last_sync`` and fetch all
210                documents regardless.  Use this to pick up deleted documents
211                or to rebuild the store from scratch.
212            on_doc_fetched: Optional callback invoked after each API page of
213                documents is fetched.  Receives ``(fetched_so_far, total)``
214                where ``total`` is the server-reported document count (may be
215                ``None`` if the server does not report it).  Use this for
216                progress reporting during the slow initial fetch.
217
218        Returns:
219            Number of documents synced.
220        """
221        from datetime import datetime, timedelta
222
223        modified_after: str | None = None
224        if not force_full:
225            conn = self._get_conn()
226            row = conn.execute(
227                "SELECT value FROM sync_metadata WHERE key = 'last_sync'"
228            ).fetchone()
229            if row is not None:
230                last_sync_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(row["value"])
231                # Subtract 1 day: modified__date__gt is date-only (strictly >),
232                # so subtracting 1 day ensures docs modified on the sync day are included.
233                cutoff = (last_sync_dt.date() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
234                modified_after = cutoff
235                logger.info("Incremental sync: fetching documents modified after %s", cutoff)
236            else:
237                logger.info("No previous sync found — performing full sync")
238        else:
239            logger.info("Force full sync requested")
240
241        logger.info("Starting sync from server")
242        docs, tags, correspondents, doc_types, storage_paths = await asyncio.gather(
243            self._client.list_documents(
244                page_size=500,
245                modified_after=modified_after,
246                on_page=on_doc_fetched,
247            ),
248            self._client.list_tags(),
249            self._client.list_correspondents(),
250            self._client.list_document_types(),
251            self._client.list_storage_paths(),
252        )
253
254        conn = self._get_conn()
255
256        # Upsert tags
257        conn.executemany(
258            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO tags (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
259            [(t.id, t.name, t.model_dump_json()) for t in tags],
260        )
261
262        # Upsert correspondents
263        conn.executemany(
264            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO correspondents (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
265            [(c.id, c.name, c.model_dump_json()) for c in correspondents],
266        )
267
268        # Upsert document types
269        conn.executemany(
270            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_types (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
271            [(dt.id, dt.name, dt.model_dump_json()) for dt in doc_types],
272        )
273
274        # Upsert storage paths
275        conn.executemany(
276            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO storage_paths (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
277            [(sp.id, sp.name, sp.model_dump_json()) for sp in storage_paths],
278        )
279
280        # Upsert documents
281        for doc in docs:
282            created_date_str  = doc.created_date.isoformat()        if doc.created_date else None
283            added_date_str    = doc.added.date().isoformat()         if doc.added         else None
284            modified_date_str = doc.modified.date().isoformat()      if doc.modified      else None
285            conn.execute(
286                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO documents
287                   (id, title, correspondent_id, document_type_id, created_date,
288                    added_date, modified_date, raw_json)
289                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
290                (
291                    doc.id,
292                    doc.title,
293                    doc.correspondent,
294                    doc.document_type,
295                    created_date_str,
296                    added_date_str,
297                    modified_date_str,
298                    doc.model_dump_json(),
299                ),
300            )
301            # Rebuild document_tags
302            conn.execute("DELETE FROM document_tags WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
303            conn.executemany(
304                "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO document_tags (document_id, tag_id) VALUES (?, ?)",
305                [(doc.id, tag_id) for tag_id in doc.tags],
306            )
307
308        from datetime import timezone
309        conn.execute(
310            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sync_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('last_sync', ?)",
311            (datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),),
312        )
313        conn.commit()
314        logger.info(
315            "Sync complete: %d documents, %d tags, %d correspondents, %d document_types, %d storage_paths",
316            len(docs), len(tags), len(correspondents), len(doc_types), len(storage_paths),
317        )
318
319        return len(docs)
320
321    async def sync_metadata(self, concurrency: int = 20) -> int:
322        """Fetch and cache file-level metadata (checksums, sizes) for all local documents.
323
324        Queries ``GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/`` for every document
325        currently in the local cache, using a semaphore to bound concurrent
326        requests.  Results are upserted into the ``document_metadata`` table.
327
328        Call this after :meth:`sync` to populate checksums needed by
329        :meth:`find_unsynced_files`.
330
331        Args:
332            concurrency: Maximum number of parallel metadata requests.
333
334        Returns:
335            Number of documents for which metadata was requested (including
336            failures, which are logged as warnings and skipped).
337        """
338        conn = self._get_conn()
339        rows = conn.execute("SELECT id FROM documents").fetchall()
340        doc_ids = [row["id"] for row in rows]
341
342        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
343
344        async def _fetch_one(doc_id: int) -> tuple[int, DocumentMetadata | None]:
345            async with sem:
346                try:
347                    meta = await self._client.get_document_metadata(doc_id)
348                    return doc_id, meta
349                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
350                    logger.warning(
351                        "Failed to fetch metadata for document %d: %s", doc_id, exc
352                    )
353                    return doc_id, None
354
355        pairs = await asyncio.gather(*[_fetch_one(doc_id) for doc_id in doc_ids])
356
357        for doc_id, meta in pairs:
358            if meta is None:
359                continue
360            conn.execute(
361                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_metadata
362                   (document_id, original_checksum, archive_checksum, original_size,
363                    archive_size, original_mime_type, media_filename,
364                    has_archive_version, raw_json)
365                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
366                (
367                    doc_id,
368                    meta.original_checksum,
369                    meta.archive_checksum,
370                    meta.original_size,
371                    meta.archive_size,
372                    meta.original_mime_type,
373                    meta.media_filename,
374                    int(meta.has_archive_version) if meta.has_archive_version is not None else None,
375                    meta.model_dump_json(),
376                ),
377            )
378
379        conn.commit()
380        logger.info("sync_metadata complete: %d documents processed", len(doc_ids))
381        return len(doc_ids)
382
383    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
384    # Checksum-based file comparison
385    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
386
387    def get_document_by_checksum(self, checksum: str) -> Document | None:
388        """Look up a document by its file checksum.
389
390        Queries the local ``document_metadata`` table for a matching
391        ``original_checksum`` or ``archive_checksum``.  Requires
392        :meth:`sync_metadata` to have been called first.
393
394        Args:
395            checksum: Hex digest string (e.g. MD5) to search for.
396
397        Returns:
398            The matching :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`,
399            or ``None`` if no match is found.
400        """
401        conn = self._get_conn()
402        row = conn.execute(
403            """SELECT d.raw_json
404               FROM document_metadata dm
405               JOIN documents d ON d.id = dm.document_id
406               WHERE dm.original_checksum = ? OR dm.archive_checksum = ?""",
407            (checksum, checksum),
408        ).fetchone()
409        if row is None:
410            return None
411        return Document.model_validate(json.loads(row["raw_json"]))
412
413    def find_unsynced_files(
414        self,
415        path: str | Path,
416        pattern: str = "**/*",
417        hash_algo: str = "md5",
418    ) -> list[Path]:
419        """Find files on disk that are not yet present in paperless.
420
421        Computes a hash for each file matched by ``pattern`` under ``path``
422        and checks it against the local ``document_metadata`` cache.  Files
423        whose hash is not found are considered unsynced.
424
425        .. note::
426            :meth:`sync_metadata` must be called before this method for
427            checksums to be present in the local database.
428
429        Args:
430            path: Root directory to search.
431            pattern: Glob pattern relative to ``path``.
432            hash_algo: Hash algorithm name accepted by :func:`hashlib.new`
433                (default ``"md5"``).
434
435        Returns:
436            List of :class:`~pathlib.Path` objects for files not found in
437            paperless.
438        """
439        root = Path(path)
440        unsynced: list[Path] = []
441
442        for file_path in root.glob(pattern):
443            if not file_path.is_file():
444                continue
445            h = hashlib.new(hash_algo)
446            with file_path.open("rb") as fh:
447                while chunk := fh.read(65536):
448                    h.update(chunk)
449            if self.get_document_by_checksum(h.hexdigest()) is None:
450                unsynced.append(file_path)
451
452        return unsynced
453
454    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
455    # Semantic search (embeddings)
456    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
457
458    async def embed_documents(
459        self,
460        provider: EmbeddingProvider,
461        *,
462        chunk_size: int = 512,
463        chunk_overlap: int = 64,
464        batch_size: int = 32,
465        force: bool = False,
466        on_chunk_embedded: Callable[[int, int], None] | None = None,
467    ) -> int:
468        """Embed all locally cached documents and store vectors in SQLite.
469
470        For each document, builds a text string from the title and OCR content,
471        splits it into overlapping chunks, and embeds each chunk via
472        ``provider``.  Embeddings are stored as ``numpy.float32`` byte blobs.
473
474        Documents that already have embeddings are skipped unless
475        ``force=True``.
476
477        Args:
478            provider: An :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider`
479                instance (e.g.
480                :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.SentenceTransformerProvider`
481                or :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.OllamaProvider`).
482            chunk_size: Maximum characters per text chunk.
483            chunk_overlap: Character overlap between consecutive chunks.
484            batch_size: Number of chunks sent to ``provider.embed()`` per call.
485            force: If ``True``, re-embed documents that already have stored
486                embeddings.
487            on_chunk_embedded: Optional callback invoked after each batch of
488                chunks is embedded and stored.  Receives
489                ``(embedded_so_far, total)`` as positional arguments.  Use
490                this for progress reporting.
491
492        Returns:
493            Total number of chunks embedded and stored.
494
495        Raises:
496            ImportError: If ``numpy`` is not installed.
497        """
498        try:
499            import numpy as np  # noqa: PLC0415
500        except ImportError as exc:
501            raise ImportError(
502                "numpy is required for embed_documents. "
503                "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings]"
504            ) from exc
505
506        conn = self._get_conn()
507        rows = conn.execute("SELECT raw_json FROM documents").fetchall()
508        docs = [Document.model_validate(json.loads(r["raw_json"])) for r in rows]
509
