510        # Collect (doc_id, chunks) pairs for documents that need embedding
511        doc_chunks: list[tuple[int, list[str]]] = []
512        for doc in docs:
513            if not force:
514                existing = conn.execute(
515                    "SELECT 1 FROM embeddings WHERE document_id = ? LIMIT 1", (doc.id,)
516                ).fetchone()
517                if existing:
518                    continue
519            else:
520                conn.execute("DELETE FROM embeddings WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
521
522            text = f"{doc.title}\n\n{doc.content or ''}"
523            chunks = _chunk_text(text, chunk_size, chunk_overlap)
524            if chunks:
525                doc_chunks.append((doc.id, chunks))
526
527        # Flatten to (doc_id, chunk_index, text) triples
528        all_items: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = [
529            (doc_id, i, chunk)
530            for doc_id, chunks in doc_chunks
531            for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks)
532        ]
533
534        total = 0
535        total_chunks = len(all_items)
536        for batch_start in range(0, total_chunks, batch_size):
537            batch = all_items[batch_start : batch_start + batch_size]
538            texts = [item[2] for item in batch]
539            embeddings = await provider.embed(texts)
540            for (doc_id, chunk_index, chunk_text_str), embedding in zip(batch, embeddings):
541                blob = np.array(embedding, dtype=np.float32).tobytes()
542                conn.execute(
543                    """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO embeddings
544                       (document_id, chunk_index, chunk_text, embedding)
545                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)""",
546                    (doc_id, chunk_index, chunk_text_str, blob),
547                )
548            total += len(batch)
549            if on_chunk_embedded is not None:
550                on_chunk_embedded(total, total_chunks)
551
552        conn.commit()
553        logger.info("embed_documents complete: %d chunks stored", total)
554        return total
555
556    async def semantic_search(
557        self,
558        query: str,
559        provider: EmbeddingProvider,
560        *,
561        max_results: int = 10,
562        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
563        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
564        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
565        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
566        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
567        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
568        document_type: int | str | None = None,
569        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
570        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
571        created_after: str | None = None,
572        created_before: str | None = None,
573        added_after: str | None = None,
574        added_before: str | None = None,
575        modified_after: str | None = None,
576        modified_before: str | None = None,
577    ) -> list[tuple[Document, float]]:
578        """Find documents semantically similar to a query string.
579
580        Embeds the query and computes cosine similarity against every stored
581        chunk embedding.  For each document the **maximum** similarity across
582        all its chunks is used (best-chunk-wins strategy).
583
584        SQL filters are applied before loading embeddings to avoid unnecessary
585        work.
586
587        Args:
588            query: Natural-language query string.
589            provider: The same
590                :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider` used
591                during :meth:`embed_documents`.
592            max_results: Maximum number of results to return.
593            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags.
594            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags.
595            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
596            correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID).
597            any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents.
598            exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents.
599            document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID).
600            any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types.
601            exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types.
602            created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date.
603            created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date.
604            added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date.
605            added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date.
606            modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date.
607            modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date.
608
609        Returns:
610            List of ``(Document, similarity_score)`` tuples, sorted by
611            similarity descending.
612
613        Raises:
614            ImportError: If ``numpy`` is not installed.
615        """
616        try:
617            import numpy as np  # noqa: PLC0415
618        except ImportError as exc:
619            raise ImportError(
620                "numpy is required for semantic_search. "
621                "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings]"
622            ) from exc
623
624        query_vecs = await provider.embed([query])
625        query_vec = np.array(query_vecs[0], dtype=np.float32)
626
627        conn = self._get_conn()
628
629        conditions, bind = self._build_filter_sql(
630            tags=tags,
631            any_tag=any_tag,
632            exclude_tags=exclude_tags,
633            correspondent=correspondent,
634            any_correspondent=any_correspondent,
635            exclude_correspondents=exclude_correspondents,
636            document_type=document_type,
637            any_document_type=any_document_type,
638            exclude_document_types=exclude_document_types,
639            created_after=created_after,
640            created_before=created_before,
641            added_after=added_after,
642            added_before=added_before,
643            modified_after=modified_after,
644            modified_before=modified_before,
645        )
646
647        if conditions:
648            where = "WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)
649            rows = conn.execute(
650                f"SELECT e.document_id, e.embedding FROM embeddings e "
651                f"JOIN documents d ON d.id = e.document_id {where}",
652                bind,
653            ).fetchall()
654        else:
655            rows = conn.execute("SELECT document_id, embedding FROM embeddings").fetchall()
656
657        if not rows:
658            return []
659
660        doc_max_sim: dict[int, float] = {}
661        for row in rows:
662            doc_id = row["document_id"]
663            vec = np.frombuffer(row["embedding"], dtype=np.float32)
664            sim = _cosine_similarity(query_vec, vec)
665            if doc_id not in doc_max_sim or sim > doc_max_sim[doc_id]:
666                doc_max_sim[doc_id] = sim
667
668        top_ids = sorted(doc_max_sim, key=doc_max_sim.__getitem__, reverse=True)[:max_results]
669
670        results: list[tuple[Document, float]] = []
671        for doc_id in top_ids:
672            row = conn.execute(
673                "SELECT raw_json FROM documents WHERE id = ?", (doc_id,)
674            ).fetchone()
675            if row:
676                doc = Document.model_validate(json.loads(row["raw_json"]))
677                results.append((doc, doc_max_sim[doc_id]))
678
679        return results
680
681    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
682    # Search (pure SQLite, no network)
683    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
684
685    def _build_filter_sql(
686        self,
687        *,
688        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
689        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
690        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
691        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
692        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
693        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
694        document_type: int | str | None = None,
695        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
696        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
697        created_after: str | None = None,
698        created_before: str | None = None,
699        added_after: str | None = None,
700        added_before: str | None = None,
701        modified_after: str | None = None,
702        modified_before: str | None = None,
703    ) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
704        """Build WHERE conditions against the ``documents d`` table alias.
705
706        Returns ``(conditions, bind)`` to be joined into a WHERE clause.
707        """
708        conditions: list[str] = []
709        bind: list[Any] = []
710
711        # tags: document must have ALL of these
712        if tags:
713            tag_ids = [self._resolve_local("tags", t) for t in tags]
714            for tag_id in tag_ids:
715                conditions.append(
716                    "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM document_tags dt"
717                    " WHERE dt.document_id = d.id AND dt.tag_id = ?)"
718                )
719                bind.append(tag_id)
720
721        # any_tag: document must have AT LEAST ONE of these
722        if any_tag:
723            tag_ids = [self._resolve_local("tags", t) for t in any_tag]
724            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(tag_ids))
725            conditions.append(
726                f"EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM document_tags dt"
727                f" WHERE dt.document_id = d.id AND dt.tag_id IN ({placeholders}))"
728            )
729            bind.extend(tag_ids)
730
731        # exclude_tags: document must have NONE of these
732        if exclude_tags:
733            tag_ids = [self._resolve_local("tags", t) for t in exclude_tags]
734            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(tag_ids))
735            conditions.append(
736                f"NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM document_tags dt"
737                f" WHERE dt.document_id = d.id AND dt.tag_id IN ({placeholders}))"
738            )
739            bind.extend(tag_ids)
740
741        # correspondent / any_correspondent — any_correspondent takes precedence
742        if any_correspondent:
743            corr_ids = [self._resolve_local("correspondents", c) for c in any_correspondent]
744            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(corr_ids))
745            conditions.append(f"d.correspondent_id IN ({placeholders})")
746            bind.extend(corr_ids)
747        elif correspondent is not None:
748            corr_id = self._resolve_local("correspondents", correspondent)
749            conditions.append("d.correspondent_id = ?")
750            bind.append(corr_id)
751
752        if exclude_correspondents:
753            corr_ids = [self._resolve_local("correspondents", c) for c in exclude_correspondents]
754            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(corr_ids))
755            conditions.append(f"(d.correspondent_id IS NULL OR d.correspondent_id NOT IN ({placeholders}))")
756            bind.extend(corr_ids)
757
758        # document_type / any_document_type — any_document_type takes precedence
759        if any_document_type:
760            dt_ids = [self._resolve_local("document_types", dt) for dt in any_document_type]
761            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(dt_ids))
762            conditions.append(f"d.document_type_id IN ({placeholders})")
763            bind.extend(dt_ids)
764        elif document_type is not None:
765            dt_id = self._resolve_local("document_types", document_type)
766            conditions.append("d.document_type_id = ?")
767            bind.append(dt_id)
768
769        if exclude_document_types:
770            dt_ids = [self._resolve_local("document_types", dt) for dt in exclude_document_types]
771            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(dt_ids))
772            conditions.append(f"(d.document_type_id IS NULL OR d.document_type_id NOT IN ({placeholders}))")
773            bind.extend(dt_ids)
774
775        # Date range filters
776        if created_after is not None:
777            conditions.append("d.created_date > ?")
778            bind.append(created_after)
779        if created_before is not None:
780            conditions.append("d.created_date < ?")
781            bind.append(created_before)
782        if added_after is not None:
783            conditions.append("d.added_date > ?")
784            bind.append(added_after)
785        if added_before is not None:
786            conditions.append("d.added_date < ?")
787            bind.append(added_before)
788        if modified_after is not None:
789            conditions.append("d.modified_date > ?")
790            bind.append(modified_after)
791        if modified_before is not None:
792            conditions.append("d.modified_date < ?")
793            bind.append(modified_before)
794
795        return conditions, bind
796
797    def search_documents(
798        self,
799        *,
800        title_contains: str | None = None,
801        title_regex: str | None = None,
802        content_regex: str | None = None,
803        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
804        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
805        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
806        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
807        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
808        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
809        document_type: int | str | None = None,
810        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
811        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
812        created_after: str | None = None,
813        created_before: str | None = None,
814        added_after: str | None = None,
815        added_before: str | None = None,
816        modified_after: str | None = None,
817        modified_before: str | None = None,
818        max_results: int | None = None,
819    ) -> list[Document]:
820        """Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.
821
822        SQL filters are applied first; ``title_regex`` and ``content_regex``
823        are applied in Python on the resulting rows.
824
825        Args:
826            title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the title.
827            title_regex: Python ``re`` pattern on the title (case-insensitive).
828            content_regex: Python ``re`` pattern on OCR content (case-insensitive).
829            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (name or ID).
830            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags.
831            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
832            correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID).
833            any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents.
834            exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents.
835            document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID).
836            any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types.
837            exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types.
838            created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date.
839            created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date.
840            added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date.
841            added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date.
842            modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date.
843            modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date.
844            max_results: If given, truncate the final result list to this length.
845
846        Returns:
847            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
848            objects matching all supplied filters.
849        """
850        logger.debug("search_documents()")
851
852        conn = self._get_conn()
853
854        conditions: list[str] = []
855        bind: list[Any] = []
856
857        if title_contains is not None:
858            conditions.append("d.title LIKE ?")
859            bind.append(f"%{title_contains}%")
860
861        filter_conditions, filter_bind = self._build_filter_sql(
862            tags=tags,
863            any_tag=any_tag,
864            exclude_tags=exclude_tags,
865            correspondent=correspondent,
866            any_correspondent=any_correspondent,
867            exclude_correspondents=exclude_correspondents,
868            document_type=document_type,
869            any_document_type=any_document_type,
870            exclude_document_types=exclude_document_types,
871            created_after=created_after,
872            created_before=created_before,
873            added_after=added_after,
874            added_before=added_before,
875            modified_after=modified_after,
876            modified_before=modified_before,
877        )
878        conditions.extend(filter_conditions)
879        bind.extend(filter_bind)
880
881        where_clause = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
882        sql = f"SELECT d.raw_json FROM documents d {where_clause}"
883        rows = conn.execute(sql, bind).fetchall()
884
885        results: list[Document] = []
886        title_re = re.compile(title_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if title_regex else None
887        content_re = re.compile(content_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if content_regex else None
888
889        for row in rows:
890            data = json.loads(row["raw_json"])
891            if title_re and not title_re.search(data.get("title", "")):
892                continue
893            if content_re and not content_re.search(data.get("content", "") or ""):
894                continue
895            results.append(Document.model_validate(data))
896
897        if max_results is not None:
898            results = results[:max_results]
899
900        logger.debug("search_documents returned %d result(s)", len(results))
901        return results
902
903    def _resolve_local(self, table: str, value: int | str) -> int:
904        if isinstance(value, int):
905            return value
906        conn = self._get_conn()
907        row = conn.execute(
908            f"SELECT id FROM {table} WHERE LOWER(name) = ?", (value.lower(),)
909        ).fetchone()
910        if row is None:
911            from easypaperless.exceptions import NotFoundError
912            raise NotFoundError(f"{table!r} item with name {value!r} not found in local cache")
913        return row["id"]

SQLite-backed local mirror of paperless-ngx document metadata.

Wraps a ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient and mirrors document metadata (plus tags, correspondents, document types, and storage paths) into a local SQLite database for fast, offline-capable search.

Sync is manual and explicit: call sync() to pull fresh data from the server. search_documents() is a pure SQLite query — no network is involved.

Example: async with PaperlessClient(url="...", api_key="...") as client: store = DocumentStore(client, "paperless.db") await store.sync() results = store.search_documents(tags=["invoice"], created_after="2024-01-01") store.close()

DocumentStore( client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | pathlib._local.Path) 
158    def __init__(self, client: PaperlessClient, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
159        """Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.
160
161        Args:
162            client: An initialised
163                :class:`~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient` used for
164                fetching data during :meth:`sync`.
165            db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.  The file is created
166                if it does not exist.
167        """
168        self._client = client
169        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
170        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None

Create a DocumentStore backed by a local SQLite file.

Args: client: An initialised ~easypaperless.client.PaperlessClient used for fetching data during sync(). db_path: Path to the SQLite database file. The file is created if it does not exist.

def close(self) -> None: 
181    def close(self) -> None:
182        """Close the SQLite database connection."""
183        if self._conn is not None:
184            self._conn.close()
185            self._conn = None

Close the SQLite database connection.

async def sync( self, *, force_full: bool = False, on_doc_fetched: Optional[Callable[[int, int | None], NoneType]] = None) -> int: 
191    async def sync(
192        self,
193        *,
194        force_full: bool = False,
195        on_doc_fetched: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None,
196    ) -> int:
197        """Pull documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.
198
199        On the first call (no ``last_sync`` recorded) a full fetch is performed.
200        On subsequent calls only documents modified after the previous sync date
201        are fetched (incremental sync), which is much faster for large archives.
202
203        Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage
204        paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite.  Existing
205        local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server
206        are *not* removed by incremental syncs.
207
208        Args:
209            force_full: If ``True``, ignore ``last_sync`` and fetch all
210                documents regardless.  Use this to pick up deleted documents
211                or to rebuild the store from scratch.
212            on_doc_fetched: Optional callback invoked after each API page of
213                documents is fetched.  Receives ``(fetched_so_far, total)``
214                where ``total`` is the server-reported document count (may be
215                ``None`` if the server does not report it).  Use this for
216                progress reporting during the slow initial fetch.
217
218        Returns:
219            Number of documents synced.
220        """
221        from datetime import datetime, timedelta
222
223        modified_after: str | None = None
224        if not force_full:
225            conn = self._get_conn()
226            row = conn.execute(
227                "SELECT value FROM sync_metadata WHERE key = 'last_sync'"
228            ).fetchone()
229            if row is not None:
230                last_sync_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(row["value"])
231                # Subtract 1 day: modified__date__gt is date-only (strictly >),
232                # so subtracting 1 day ensures docs modified on the sync day are included.
233                cutoff = (last_sync_dt.date() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
234                modified_after = cutoff
235                logger.info("Incremental sync: fetching documents modified after %s", cutoff)
236            else:
237                logger.info("No previous sync found — performing full sync")
238        else:
239            logger.info("Force full sync requested")
240
241        logger.info("Starting sync from server")
242        docs, tags, correspondents, doc_types, storage_paths = await asyncio.gather(
243            self._client.list_documents(
244                page_size=500,
245                modified_after=modified_after,
246                on_page=on_doc_fetched,
247            ),
248            self._client.list_tags(),
249            self._client.list_correspondents(),
250            self._client.list_document_types(),
251            self._client.list_storage_paths(),
252        )
253
254        conn = self._get_conn()
255
256        # Upsert tags
257        conn.executemany(
258            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO tags (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
259            [(t.id, t.name, t.model_dump_json()) for t in tags],
260        )
261
262        # Upsert correspondents
263        conn.executemany(
264            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO correspondents (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
265            [(c.id, c.name, c.model_dump_json()) for c in correspondents],
266        )
267
268        # Upsert document types
269        conn.executemany(
270            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_types (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
271            [(dt.id, dt.name, dt.model_dump_json()) for dt in doc_types],
272        )
273
274        # Upsert storage paths
275        conn.executemany(
276            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO storage_paths (id, name, raw_json) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
277            [(sp.id, sp.name, sp.model_dump_json()) for sp in storage_paths],
278        )
279
280        # Upsert documents
281        for doc in docs:
282            created_date_str  = doc.created_date.isoformat()        if doc.created_date else None
283            added_date_str    = doc.added.date().isoformat()         if doc.added         else None
284            modified_date_str = doc.modified.date().isoformat()      if doc.modified      else None
285            conn.execute(
286                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO documents
287                   (id, title, correspondent_id, document_type_id, created_date,
288                    added_date, modified_date, raw_json)
289                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
290                (
291                    doc.id,
292                    doc.title,
293                    doc.correspondent,
294                    doc.document_type,
295                    created_date_str,
296                    added_date_str,
297                    modified_date_str,
298                    doc.model_dump_json(),
299                ),
300            )
301            # Rebuild document_tags
302            conn.execute("DELETE FROM document_tags WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
303            conn.executemany(
304                "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO document_tags (document_id, tag_id) VALUES (?, ?)",
305                [(doc.id, tag_id) for tag_id in doc.tags],
306            )
307
308        from datetime import timezone
309        conn.execute(
310            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sync_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('last_sync', ?)",
311            (datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),),
312        )
313        conn.commit()
314        logger.info(
315            "Sync complete: %d documents, %d tags, %d correspondents, %d document_types, %d storage_paths",
316            len(docs), len(tags), len(correspondents), len(doc_types), len(storage_paths),
317        )
318
319        return len(docs)

Pull documents and supporting metadata from the server and upsert locally.

On the first call (no last_sync recorded) a full fetch is performed. On subsequent calls only documents modified after the previous sync date are fetched (incremental sync), which is much faster for large archives.

Fetches documents, tags, correspondents, document types, and storage paths in parallel, then upserts everything into SQLite. Existing local records are replaced; documents no longer present on the server are not removed by incremental syncs.

Args: force_full: If True, ignore last_sync and fetch all documents regardless. Use this to pick up deleted documents or to rebuild the store from scratch. on_doc_fetched: Optional callback invoked after each API page of documents is fetched. Receives (fetched_so_far, total) where total is the server-reported document count (may be None if the server does not report it). Use this for progress reporting during the slow initial fetch.

Returns: Number of documents synced.

async def sync_metadata(self, concurrency: int = 20) -> int: 
321    async def sync_metadata(self, concurrency: int = 20) -> int:
322        """Fetch and cache file-level metadata (checksums, sizes) for all local documents.
323
324        Queries ``GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/`` for every document
325        currently in the local cache, using a semaphore to bound concurrent
326        requests.  Results are upserted into the ``document_metadata`` table.
327
328        Call this after :meth:`sync` to populate checksums needed by
329        :meth:`find_unsynced_files`.
330
331        Args:
332            concurrency: Maximum number of parallel metadata requests.
333
334        Returns:
335            Number of documents for which metadata was requested (including
336            failures, which are logged as warnings and skipped).
337        """
338        conn = self._get_conn()
339        rows = conn.execute("SELECT id FROM documents").fetchall()
340        doc_ids = [row["id"] for row in rows]
341
342        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
343
344        async def _fetch_one(doc_id: int) -> tuple[int, DocumentMetadata | None]:
345            async with sem:
346                try:
347                    meta = await self._client.get_document_metadata(doc_id)
348                    return doc_id, meta
349                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
350                    logger.warning(
351                        "Failed to fetch metadata for document %d: %s", doc_id, exc
352                    )
353                    return doc_id, None
354
355        pairs = await asyncio.gather(*[_fetch_one(doc_id) for doc_id in doc_ids])
356
357        for doc_id, meta in pairs:
358            if meta is None:
359                continue
360            conn.execute(
361                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO document_metadata
362                   (document_id, original_checksum, archive_checksum, original_size,
363                    archive_size, original_mime_type, media_filename,
364                    has_archive_version, raw_json)
365                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
366                (
367                    doc_id,
368                    meta.original_checksum,
369                    meta.archive_checksum,
370                    meta.original_size,
371                    meta.archive_size,
372                    meta.original_mime_type,
373                    meta.media_filename,
374                    int(meta.has_archive_version) if meta.has_archive_version is not None else None,
375                    meta.model_dump_json(),
376                ),
377            )
378
379        conn.commit()
380        logger.info("sync_metadata complete: %d documents processed", len(doc_ids))
381        return len(doc_ids)

Fetch and cache file-level metadata (checksums, sizes) for all local documents.

Queries GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/ for every document currently in the local cache, using a semaphore to bound concurrent requests. Results are upserted into the document_metadata table.

Call this after sync() to populate checksums needed by find_unsynced_files().

Args: concurrency: Maximum number of parallel metadata requests.

Returns: Number of documents for which metadata was requested (including failures, which are logged as warnings and skipped).

def get_document_by_checksum(self, checksum: str) -> Document | None: 
387    def get_document_by_checksum(self, checksum: str) -> Document | None:
388        """Look up a document by its file checksum.
389
390        Queries the local ``document_metadata`` table for a matching
391        ``original_checksum`` or ``archive_checksum``.  Requires
392        :meth:`sync_metadata` to have been called first.
393
394        Args:
395            checksum: Hex digest string (e.g. MD5) to search for.
396
397        Returns:
398            The matching :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`,
399            or ``None`` if no match is found.
400        """
401        conn = self._get_conn()
402        row = conn.execute(
403            """SELECT d.raw_json
404               FROM document_metadata dm
405               JOIN documents d ON d.id = dm.document_id
406               WHERE dm.original_checksum = ? OR dm.archive_checksum = ?""",
407            (checksum, checksum),
408        ).fetchone()
409        if row is None:
410            return None
411        return Document.model_validate(json.loads(row["raw_json"]))

Look up a document by its file checksum.

Queries the local document_metadata table for a matching original_checksum or archive_checksum. Requires sync_metadata() to have been called first.

Args: checksum: Hex digest string (e.g. MD5) to search for.

Returns: The matching ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document, or None if no match is found.

def find_unsynced_files( self, path: str | pathlib._local.Path, pattern: str = '**/*', hash_algo: str = 'md5') -> list[pathlib._local.Path]: 
413    def find_unsynced_files(
414        self,
415        path: str | Path,
416        pattern: str = "**/*",
417        hash_algo: str = "md5",
418    ) -> list[Path]:
419        """Find files on disk that are not yet present in paperless.
420
421        Computes a hash for each file matched by ``pattern`` under ``path``
422        and checks it against the local ``document_metadata`` cache.  Files
423        whose hash is not found are considered unsynced.
424
425        .. note::
426            :meth:`sync_metadata` must be called before this method for
427            checksums to be present in the local database.
428
429        Args:
430            path: Root directory to search.
431            pattern: Glob pattern relative to ``path``.
432            hash_algo: Hash algorithm name accepted by :func:`hashlib.new`
433                (default ``"md5"``).
434
435        Returns:
436            List of :class:`~pathlib.Path` objects for files not found in
437            paperless.
438        """
439        root = Path(path)
440        unsynced: list[Path] = []
441
442        for file_path in root.glob(pattern):
443            if not file_path.is_file():
444                continue
445            h = hashlib.new(hash_algo)
446            with file_path.open("rb") as fh:
447                while chunk := fh.read(65536):
448                    h.update(chunk)
449            if self.get_document_by_checksum(h.hexdigest()) is None:
450                unsynced.append(file_path)
451
452        return unsynced

Find files on disk that are not yet present in paperless.

Computes a hash for each file matched by pattern under path and checks it against the local document_metadata cache. Files whose hash is not found are considered unsynced.

checksums to be present in the local database.

Args: path: Root directory to search. pattern: Glob pattern relative to path. hash_algo: Hash algorithm name accepted by hashlib.new() (default "md5").

Returns: List of ~pathlib.Path objects for files not found in paperless.

async def embed_documents( self, provider: easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider, *, chunk_size: int = 512, chunk_overlap: int = 64, batch_size: int = 32, force: bool = False, on_chunk_embedded: Optional[Callable[[int, int], NoneType]] = None) -> int: 
458    async def embed_documents(
459        self,
460        provider: EmbeddingProvider,
461        *,
462        chunk_size: int = 512,
463        chunk_overlap: int = 64,
464        batch_size: int = 32,
465        force: bool = False,
466        on_chunk_embedded: Callable[[int, int], None] | None = None,
467    ) -> int:
468        """Embed all locally cached documents and store vectors in SQLite.
469
470        For each document, builds a text string from the title and OCR content,
471        splits it into overlapping chunks, and embeds each chunk via
472        ``provider``.  Embeddings are stored as ``numpy.float32`` byte blobs.
473
474        Documents that already have embeddings are skipped unless
475        ``force=True``.
476
477        Args:
478            provider: An :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider`
479                instance (e.g.
480                :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.SentenceTransformerProvider`
481                or :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.OllamaProvider`).
482            chunk_size: Maximum characters per text chunk.
483            chunk_overlap: Character overlap between consecutive chunks.
484            batch_size: Number of chunks sent to ``provider.embed()`` per call.
485            force: If ``True``, re-embed documents that already have stored
486                embeddings.
487            on_chunk_embedded: Optional callback invoked after each batch of
488                chunks is embedded and stored.  Receives
489                ``(embedded_so_far, total)`` as positional arguments.  Use
490                this for progress reporting.
491
492        Returns:
493            Total number of chunks embedded and stored.
494
495        Raises:
496            ImportError: If ``numpy`` is not installed.
497        """
498        try:
499            import numpy as np  # noqa: PLC0415
500        except ImportError as exc:
501            raise ImportError(
502                "numpy is required for embed_documents. "
503                "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings]"
504            ) from exc
505
506        conn = self._get_conn()
507        rows = conn.execute("SELECT raw_json FROM documents").fetchall()
508        docs = [Document.model_validate(json.loads(r["raw_json"])) for r in rows]
509
510        # Collect (doc_id, chunks) pairs for documents that need embedding
511        doc_chunks: list[tuple[int, list[str]]] = []
512        for doc in docs:
513            if not force:
514                existing = conn.execute(
515                    "SELECT 1 FROM embeddings WHERE document_id = ? LIMIT 1", (doc.id,)
516                ).fetchone()
517                if existing:
518                    continue
519            else:
520                conn.execute("DELETE FROM embeddings WHERE document_id = ?", (doc.id,))
521
522            text = f"{doc.title}\n\n{doc.content or ''}"
523            chunks = _chunk_text(text, chunk_size, chunk_overlap)
524            if chunks:
525                doc_chunks.append((doc.id, chunks))
526
527        # Flatten to (doc_id, chunk_index, text) triples
528        all_items: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = [
529            (doc_id, i, chunk)
530            for doc_id, chunks in doc_chunks
531            for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks)
532        ]
533
534        total = 0
535        total_chunks = len(all_items)
536        for batch_start in range(0, total_chunks, batch_size):
537            batch = all_items[batch_start : batch_start + batch_size]
538            texts = [item[2] for item in batch]
539            embeddings = await provider.embed(texts)
540            for (doc_id, chunk_index, chunk_text_str), embedding in zip(batch, embeddings):
541                blob = np.array(embedding, dtype=np.float32).tobytes()
542                conn.execute(
543                    """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO embeddings
544                       (document_id, chunk_index, chunk_text, embedding)
545                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)""",
546                    (doc_id, chunk_index, chunk_text_str, blob),
547                )
548            total += len(batch)
549            if on_chunk_embedded is not None:
550                on_chunk_embedded(total, total_chunks)
551
552        conn.commit()
553        logger.info("embed_documents complete: %d chunks stored", total)
554        return total

Embed all locally cached documents and store vectors in SQLite.

For each document, builds a text string from the title and OCR content, splits it into overlapping chunks, and embeds each chunk via provider. Embeddings are stored as numpy.float32 byte blobs.

Documents that already have embeddings are skipped unless force=True.

Args: provider: An ~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider instance (e.g. ~easypaperless._embedding.SentenceTransformerProvider or ~easypaperless._embedding.OllamaProvider). chunk_size: Maximum characters per text chunk. chunk_overlap: Character overlap between consecutive chunks. batch_size: Number of chunks sent to provider.embed() per call. force: If True, re-embed documents that already have stored embeddings. on_chunk_embedded: Optional callback invoked after each batch of chunks is embedded and stored. Receives (embedded_so_far, total) as positional arguments. Use this for progress reporting.

Returns: Total number of chunks embedded and stored.

Raises: ImportError: If numpy is not installed.

async def semantic_search( self, query: str, provider: easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider, *, max_results: int = 10, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None, created_after: str | None = None, created_before: str | None = None, added_after: str | None = None, added_before: str | None = None, modified_after: str | None = None, modified_before: str | None = None) -> list[tuple[Document, float]]: 
556    async def semantic_search(
557        self,
558        query: str,
559        provider: EmbeddingProvider,
560        *,
561        max_results: int = 10,
562        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
563        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
564        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
565        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
566        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
567        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
568        document_type: int | str | None = None,
569        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
570        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
571        created_after: str | None = None,
572        created_before: str | None = None,
573        added_after: str | None = None,
574        added_before: str | None = None,
575        modified_after: str | None = None,
576        modified_before: str | None = None,
577    ) -> list[tuple[Document, float]]:
578        """Find documents semantically similar to a query string.
579
580        Embeds the query and computes cosine similarity against every stored
581        chunk embedding.  For each document the **maximum** similarity across
582        all its chunks is used (best-chunk-wins strategy).
583
584        SQL filters are applied before loading embeddings to avoid unnecessary
585        work.
586
587        Args:
588            query: Natural-language query string.
589            provider: The same
590                :class:`~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider` used
591                during :meth:`embed_documents`.
592            max_results: Maximum number of results to return.
593            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags.
594            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags.
595            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
596            correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID).
597            any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents.
598            exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents.
599            document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID).
600            any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types.
601            exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types.
602            created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date.
603            created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date.
604            added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date.
605            added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date.
606            modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date.
607            modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date.
608
609        Returns:
610            List of ``(Document, similarity_score)`` tuples, sorted by
611            similarity descending.
612
613        Raises:
614            ImportError: If ``numpy`` is not installed.
615        """
616        try:
617            import numpy as np  # noqa: PLC0415
618        except ImportError as exc:
619            raise ImportError(
620                "numpy is required for semantic_search. "
621                "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings]"
622            ) from exc
623
624        query_vecs = await provider.embed([query])
625        query_vec = np.array(query_vecs[0], dtype=np.float32)
626
627        conn = self._get_conn()
628
629        conditions, bind = self._build_filter_sql(
630            tags=tags,
631            any_tag=any_tag,
632            exclude_tags=exclude_tags,
633            correspondent=correspondent,
634            any_correspondent=any_correspondent,
635            exclude_correspondents=exclude_correspondents,
636            document_type=document_type,
637            any_document_type=any_document_type,
638            exclude_document_types=exclude_document_types,
639            created_after=created_after,
640            created_before=created_before,
641            added_after=added_after,
642            added_before=added_before,
643            modified_after=modified_after,
644            modified_before=modified_before,
645        )
646
647        if conditions:
648            where = "WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)
649            rows = conn.execute(
650                f"SELECT e.document_id, e.embedding FROM embeddings e "
651                f"JOIN documents d ON d.id = e.document_id {where}",
652                bind,
653            ).fetchall()
654        else:
655            rows = conn.execute("SELECT document_id, embedding FROM embeddings").fetchall()
656
657        if not rows:
658            return []
659
660        doc_max_sim: dict[int, float] = {}
661        for row in rows:
662            doc_id = row["document_id"]
663            vec = np.frombuffer(row["embedding"], dtype=np.float32)
664            sim = _cosine_similarity(query_vec, vec)
665            if doc_id not in doc_max_sim or sim > doc_max_sim[doc_id]:
666                doc_max_sim[doc_id] = sim
667
668        top_ids = sorted(doc_max_sim, key=doc_max_sim.__getitem__, reverse=True)[:max_results]
669
670        results: list[tuple[Document, float]] = []
671        for doc_id in top_ids:
672            row = conn.execute(
673                "SELECT raw_json FROM documents WHERE id = ?", (doc_id,)
674            ).fetchone()
675            if row:
676                doc = Document.model_validate(json.loads(row["raw_json"]))
677                results.append((doc, doc_max_sim[doc_id]))
678
679        return results

Find documents semantically similar to a query string.

Embeds the query and computes cosine similarity against every stored chunk embedding. For each document the maximum similarity across all its chunks is used (best-chunk-wins strategy).

SQL filters are applied before loading embeddings to avoid unnecessary work.

Args: query: Natural-language query string. provider: The same ~easypaperless._embedding.EmbeddingProvider used during embed_documents(). max_results: Maximum number of results to return. tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags. any_tag: Documents must have at least one of these tags. exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags. correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID). any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents. exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents. document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID). any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types. exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types. created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date. created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date. added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date. added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date. modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date. modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date.

Returns: List of (Document, similarity_score) tuples, sorted by similarity descending.

Raises: ImportError: If numpy is not installed.

def search_documents( self, *, title_contains: str | None = None, title_regex: str | None = None, content_regex: str | None = None, tags: list[int | str] | None = None, any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None = None, any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None, document_type: int | str | None = None, any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None, exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None, created_after: str | None = None, created_before: str | None = None, added_after: str | None = None, added_before: str | None = None, modified_after: str | None = None, modified_before: str | None = None, max_results: int | None = None) -> list[Document]: 
797    def search_documents(
798        self,
799        *,
800        title_contains: str | None = None,
801        title_regex: str | None = None,
802        content_regex: str | None = None,
803        tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
804        any_tag: list[int | str] | None = None,
805        exclude_tags: list[int | str] | None = None,
806        correspondent: int | str | None = None,
807        any_correspondent: list[int | str] | None = None,
808        exclude_correspondents: list[int | str] | None = None,
809        document_type: int | str | None = None,
810        any_document_type: list[int | str] | None = None,
811        exclude_document_types: list[int | str] | None = None,
812        created_after: str | None = None,
813        created_before: str | None = None,
814        added_after: str | None = None,
815        added_before: str | None = None,
816        modified_after: str | None = None,
817        modified_before: str | None = None,
818        max_results: int | None = None,
819    ) -> list[Document]:
820        """Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.
821
822        SQL filters are applied first; ``title_regex`` and ``content_regex``
823        are applied in Python on the resulting rows.
824
825        Args:
826            title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the title.
827            title_regex: Python ``re`` pattern on the title (case-insensitive).
828            content_regex: Python ``re`` pattern on OCR content (case-insensitive).
829            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (name or ID).
830            any_tag: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags.
831            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
832            correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID).
833            any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents.
834            exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents.
835            document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID).
836            any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types.
837            exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types.
838            created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date.
839            created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date.
840            added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date.
841            added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date.
842            modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date.
843            modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date.
844            max_results: If given, truncate the final result list to this length.
845
846        Returns:
847            List of :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
848            objects matching all supplied filters.
849        """
850        logger.debug("search_documents()")
851
852        conn = self._get_conn()
853
854        conditions: list[str] = []
855        bind: list[Any] = []
856
857        if title_contains is not None:
858            conditions.append("d.title LIKE ?")
859            bind.append(f"%{title_contains}%")
860
861        filter_conditions, filter_bind = self._build_filter_sql(
862            tags=tags,
863            any_tag=any_tag,
864            exclude_tags=exclude_tags,
865            correspondent=correspondent,
866            any_correspondent=any_correspondent,
867            exclude_correspondents=exclude_correspondents,
868            document_type=document_type,
869            any_document_type=any_document_type,
870            exclude_document_types=exclude_document_types,
871            created_after=created_after,
872            created_before=created_before,
873            added_after=added_after,
874            added_before=added_before,
875            modified_after=modified_after,
876            modified_before=modified_before,
877        )
878        conditions.extend(filter_conditions)
879        bind.extend(filter_bind)
880
881        where_clause = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
882        sql = f"SELECT d.raw_json FROM documents d {where_clause}"
883        rows = conn.execute(sql, bind).fetchall()
884
885        results: list[Document] = []
886        title_re = re.compile(title_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if title_regex else None
887        content_re = re.compile(content_regex, re.IGNORECASE) if content_regex else None
888
889        for row in rows:
890            data = json.loads(row["raw_json"])
891            if title_re and not title_re.search(data.get("title", "")):
892                continue
893            if content_re and not content_re.search(data.get("content", "") or ""):
894                continue
895            results.append(Document.model_validate(data))
896
897        if max_results is not None:
898            results = results[:max_results]
899
900        logger.debug("search_documents returned %d result(s)", len(results))
901        return results

Search the local SQLite cache — no network request is made.

SQL filters are applied first; title_regex and content_regex are applied in Python on the resulting rows.

Args: title_contains: Case-insensitive substring match on the title. title_regex: Python re pattern on the title (case-insensitive). content_regex: Python re pattern on OCR content (case-insensitive). tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (name or ID). any_tag: Documents must have at least one of these tags. exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags. correspondent: Filter to a single correspondent (name or ID). any_correspondent: Filter to any of these correspondents. exclude_correspondents: Exclude these correspondents. document_type: Filter to a single document type (name or ID). any_document_type: Filter to any of these document types. exclude_document_types: Exclude these document types. created_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs created after this date. created_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs created before this date. added_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs added after this date. added_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs added before this date. modified_after: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified after this date. modified_before: ISO-8601 date — only docs modified before this date. max_results: If given, truncate the final result list to this length.

Returns: List of ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document objects matching all supplied filters.

class OllamaProvider: 
 69class OllamaProvider:
 70    """Embedding provider backed by a local Ollama instance.
 71
 72    Uses the ``/api/embed`` endpoint which accepts a list of inputs in one
 73    request.  No extra dependencies are required beyond ``httpx``.
 74
 75    Args:
 76        model: Ollama model name (e.g. ``"nomic-embed-text"``).
 77        url: Base URL of the Ollama server.
 78        timeout: Request timeout in seconds.  Defaults to 120 s to allow for
 79            cold model loading.
 80    """
 81
 82    def __init__(
 83        self,
 84        model: str,
 85        url: str = "http://localhost:11434",
 86        timeout: float = 120.0,
 87    ) -> None:
 88        self._model = model
 89        self._url = url.rstrip("/")
 90        self._timeout = timeout
 91
 92    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
 93        """Embed texts via the Ollama ``/api/embed`` endpoint.
 94
 95        Args:
 96            texts: List of strings to embed.
 97
 98        Returns:
 99            List of embedding vectors (one per input string).
100
101        Raises:
102            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the Ollama server returns an error.
103        """
104        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self._timeout) as client:
105            resp = await client.post(
106                f"{self._url}/api/embed",
107                json={"model": self._model, "input": texts},
108            )
109            resp.raise_for_status()
110            return resp.json()["embeddings"]

Embedding provider backed by a local Ollama instance.

Uses the /api/embed endpoint which accepts a list of inputs in one request. No extra dependencies are required beyond httpx.

Args: model: Ollama model name (e.g. "nomic-embed-text"). url: Base URL of the Ollama server. timeout: Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 120 s to allow for cold model loading.

OllamaProvider( model: str, url: str = 'http://localhost:11434', timeout: float = 120.0) 
82    def __init__(
83        self,
84        model: str,
85        url: str = "http://localhost:11434",
86        timeout: float = 120.0,
87    ) -> None:
88        self._model = model
89        self._url = url.rstrip("/")
90        self._timeout = timeout
async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: 
 92    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
 93        """Embed texts via the Ollama ``/api/embed`` endpoint.
 94
 95        Args:
 96            texts: List of strings to embed.
 97
 98        Returns:
 99            List of embedding vectors (one per input string).
100
101        Raises:
102            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the Ollama server returns an error.
103        """
104        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self._timeout) as client:
105            resp = await client.post(
106                f"{self._url}/api/embed",
107                json={"model": self._model, "input": texts},
108            )
109            resp.raise_for_status()
110            return resp.json()["embeddings"]

Embed texts via the Ollama /api/embed endpoint.

Args: texts: List of strings to embed.

Returns: List of embedding vectors (one per input string).

Raises: httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the Ollama server returns an error.

class SentenceTransformerProvider: 
20class SentenceTransformerProvider:
21    """Embedding provider backed by sentence-transformers (local model).
22
23    Requires the ``sentence-transformers`` package::
24
25        pip install easypaperless[embeddings-st]
26
27    The model is lazy-loaded on the first :meth:`embed` call.
28
29    Args:
30        model: Sentence-transformers model name.
31    """
32
33    def __init__(self, model: str = "paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2") -> None:
34        self._model_name = model
35        self._model = None
36
37    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
38        """Embed texts using the sentence-transformers model.
39
40        The underlying ``model.encode()`` call is CPU-bound and is run in a
41        thread pool to avoid blocking the event loop.
42
43        Args:
44            texts: List of strings to embed.
45
46        Returns:
47            List of embedding vectors (one per input string).
48
49        Raises:
50            ImportError: If ``sentence-transformers`` is not installed.
51        """
52        if self._model is None:
53            try:
54                from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
55            except ImportError as exc:
56                raise ImportError(
57                    "sentence-transformers is required for SentenceTransformerProvider. "
58                    "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings-st]"
59                ) from exc
60            loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
61            self._model = await loop.run_in_executor(
62                None, SentenceTransformer, self._model_name
63            )
64        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
65        embeddings = await loop.run_in_executor(None, self._model.encode, texts)
66        return [e.tolist() for e in embeddings]

Embedding provider backed by sentence-transformers (local model).

Requires the sentence-transformers package::

pip install easypaperless[embeddings-st]

The model is lazy-loaded on the first embed() call.

Args: model: Sentence-transformers model name.

SentenceTransformerProvider(model: str = 'paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2') 
33    def __init__(self, model: str = "paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2") -> None:
34        self._model_name = model
35        self._model = None
async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: 
37    async def embed(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
38        """Embed texts using the sentence-transformers model.
39
40        The underlying ``model.encode()`` call is CPU-bound and is run in a
41        thread pool to avoid blocking the event loop.
42
43        Args:
44            texts: List of strings to embed.
45
46        Returns:
47            List of embedding vectors (one per input string).
48
49        Raises:
50            ImportError: If ``sentence-transformers`` is not installed.
51        """
52        if self._model is None:
53            try:
54                from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
55            except ImportError as exc:
56                raise ImportError(
57                    "sentence-transformers is required for SentenceTransformerProvider. "
58                    "Install it with: pip install easypaperless[embeddings-st]"
59                ) from exc
60            loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
61            self._model = await loop.run_in_executor(
62                None, SentenceTransformer, self._model_name
63            )
64        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
65        embeddings = await loop.run_in_executor(None, self._model.encode, texts)
66        return [e.tolist() for e in embeddings]

Embed texts using the sentence-transformers model.

The underlying model.encode() call is CPU-bound and is run in a thread pool to avoid blocking the event loop.

Args: texts: List of strings to embed.

Returns: List of embedding vectors (one per input string).

Raises: ImportError: If sentence-transformers is not installed.

class Correspondent(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
11class Correspondent(BaseModel):
12    """A paperless-ngx correspondent (sender or recipient of a document).
13
14    Attributes:
15        id: Unique correspondent ID.
16        name: Correspondent name.
17        document_count: Number of documents assigned to this correspondent.
18        last_correspondence: Date of the most recent document assigned here,
19            or ``None``.
20    """
21
22    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
23
24    id: int
25    name: str
26    slug: str | None = None
27    match: str | None = None
28    matching_algorithm: int | None = None
29    is_insensitive: bool | None = None
30    document_count: int | None = None
31    last_correspondence: date | None = None
32    owner: int | None = None
33    user_can_change: bool | None = None

A paperless-ngx correspondent (sender or recipient of a document).

Attributes: id: Unique correspondent ID. name: Correspondent name. document_count: Number of documents assigned to this correspondent. last_correspondence: Date of the most recent document assigned here, or None.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
slug: str | None = None
match: str | None = None
matching_algorithm: int | None = None
is_insensitive: bool | None = None
document_count: int | None = None
last_correspondence: datetime.date | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
class CustomField(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
26class CustomField(BaseModel):
27    """A custom field definition in paperless-ngx.
28
29    Attributes:
30        id: Unique custom-field ID.
31        name: Field name shown in the UI.
32        data_type: The value type for this field (see
33            :class:`FieldDataType`).
34        extra_data: Additional configuration (e.g. select options), or
35            ``None``.
36        document_count: Number of documents that have a value for this field.
37    """
38
39    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
40
41    id: int
42    name: str
43    data_type: FieldDataType
44    extra_data: Any = None
45    document_count: int | None = None

A custom field definition in paperless-ngx.

Attributes: id: Unique custom-field ID. name: Field name shown in the UI. data_type: The value type for this field (see FieldDataType). extra_data: Additional configuration (e.g. select options), or None. document_count: Number of documents that have a value for this field.

id: int = PydanticUndefined
name: str = PydanticUndefined
data_type: FieldDataType = PydanticUndefined
extra_data: Any = None
document_count: int | None = None
class CustomFieldValue(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
66class CustomFieldValue(BaseModel):
67    """A custom field value attached to a document.
68
69    Attributes:
70        field: ID of the :class:`~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField`
71            definition.
72        value: The stored value; its Python type depends on the field's
73            ``data_type``.
74    """
75
76    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
77
78    field: int
79    value: Any = None

A custom field value attached to a document.

Attributes: field: ID of the ~easypaperless.models.custom_fields.CustomField definition. value: The stored value; its Python type depends on the field's data_type.

field: int = PydanticUndefined
value: Any = None
class Document(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
172class Document(BaseModel):
173    """A paperless-ngx document.
174
175    Attributes:
176        id: Unique document ID.
177        title: Document title.
178        content: Full OCR text content, if available.
179        tags: List of tag IDs assigned to this document.
180        document_type: ID of the assigned document type, or ``None``.
181        correspondent: ID of the assigned correspondent, or ``None``.
182        storage_path: ID of the assigned storage path, or ``None``.
183        created: Full creation datetime.
184        created_date: Date portion of creation (``date`` object).
185        archive_serial_number: Archive serial number (ASN), or ``None``.
186        custom_fields: List of :class:`CustomFieldValue` instances.
187        notes: User notes attached to this document.
188        search_hit: Full-text search relevance metadata, populated only when
189            the document was returned by a full-text search.
190        metadata: Extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type).
191            ``None`` unless the document was fetched with
192            ``include_metadata=True`` or enriched via
193            :meth:`~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document_metadata`.
194    """
195
196    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
197
198    id: int
199    title: str
200    content: str | None = None
201    tags: list[int] = Field(default_factory=list)
202    document_type: int | None = None
203    correspondent: int | None = None
204    storage_path: int | None = None
205    created: datetime | None = None
206    created_date: date | None = None
207    modified: datetime | None = None
208    added: datetime | None = None
209    archive_serial_number: int | None = None
210    original_file_name: str | None = None
211    archived_file_name: str | None = None
212    owner: int | None = None
213    user_can_change: bool | None = None
214    is_shared_by_requester: bool | None = None
215    notes: list[DocumentNote] = Field(default_factory=list)
216    custom_fields: list[CustomFieldValue] = Field(default_factory=list)
217    search_hit: SearchHit | None = Field(default=None, alias="__search_hit__")
218    metadata: DocumentMetadata | None = None

A paperless-ngx document.

Attributes: id: Unique document ID. title: Document title. content: Full OCR text content, if available. tags: List of tag IDs assigned to this document. document_type: ID of the assigned document type, or None. correspondent: ID of the assigned correspondent, or None. storage_path: ID of the assigned storage path, or None. created: Full creation datetime. created_date: Date portion of creation (date object). archive_serial_number: Archive serial number (ASN), or None. custom_fields: List of CustomFieldValue instances. notes: User notes attached to this document. search_hit: Full-text search relevance metadata, populated only when the document was returned by a full-text search. metadata: Extended file-level metadata (checksums, sizes, MIME type). None unless the document was fetched with include_metadata=True or enriched via ~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document_metadata().

id: int = PydanticUndefined
title: str = PydanticUndefined
content: str | None = None
tags: list[int] = PydanticUndefined
document_type: int | None = None
correspondent: int | None = None
storage_path: int | None = None
created: datetime.datetime | None = None
created_date: datetime.date | None = None
modified: datetime.datetime | None = None
added: datetime.datetime | None = None
archive_serial_number: int | None = None
original_file_name: str | None = None
archived_file_name: str | None = None
owner: int | None = None
user_can_change: bool | None = None
is_shared_by_requester: bool | None = None
notes: list[DocumentNote] = PydanticUndefined
custom_fields: list[CustomFieldValue] = PydanticUndefined
search_hit: SearchHit | None = None
metadata: DocumentMetadata | None = None
class DocumentMetadata(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
131class DocumentMetadata(BaseModel):
132    """Extended file-level metadata for a document.
133
134    Returned by ``GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/`` and optionally attached
135    to a :class:`Document` when :meth:`~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document`
136    is called with ``include_metadata=True``.
137
138    Because reading metadata requires disk I/O it is **not** included in
139    document list responses; it must be requested explicitly.
140
141    Attributes:
142        original_checksum: MD5 checksum of the original uploaded file.
143        original_size: Size of the original file in bytes.
144        original_mime_type: MIME type of the original file
145            (e.g. ``"application/pdf"``).
146        media_filename: Path of the archived file relative to the
147            paperless-ngx media root.
148        has_archive_version: ``True`` when paperless-ngx has produced an
149            archived (post-processed) PDF in addition to the original.
150        original_metadata: File-level metadata entries extracted from the
151            original document (PDF XMP/info tags, etc.).
152        archive_checksum: MD5 checksum of the archived file, or ``None`` if
153            no archive version exists.
154        archive_size: Size of the archived file in bytes, or ``None``.
155        archive_metadata: File-level metadata entries from the archived
156            document, or ``None``.
157    """
158
159    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
160
161    original_checksum: str | None = None
162    original_size: int | None = None
163    original_mime_type: str | None = None
164    media_filename: str | None = None
165    has_archive_version: bool | None = None
166    original_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] = Field(default_factory=list)
167    archive_checksum: str | None = None
168    archive_size: int | None = None
169    archive_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] | None = None

Extended file-level metadata for a document.

Returned by GET /api/documents/{id}/metadata/ and optionally attached to a Document when ~easypaperless.PaperlessClient.get_document() is called with include_metadata=True.

Because reading metadata requires disk I/O it is not included in document list responses; it must be requested explicitly.

Attributes: original_checksum: MD5 checksum of the original uploaded file. original_size: Size of the original file in bytes. original_mime_type: MIME type of the original file (e.g. "application/pdf"). media_filename: Path of the archived file relative to the paperless-ngx media root. has_archive_version: True when paperless-ngx has produced an archived (post-processed) PDF in addition to the original. original_metadata: File-level metadata entries extracted from the original document (PDF XMP/info tags, etc.). archive_checksum: MD5 checksum of the archived file, or None if no archive version exists. archive_size: Size of the archived file in bytes, or None. archive_metadata: File-level metadata entries from the archived document, or None.

original_checksum: str | None = None
original_size: int | None = None
original_mime_type: str | None = None
media_filename: str | None = None
has_archive_version: bool | None = None
original_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] = PydanticUndefined
archive_checksum: str | None = None
archive_size: int | None = None
archive_metadata: list[FileMetadataEntry] | None = None
class DocumentNote(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 82class DocumentNote(BaseModel):
 83    """A user note attached to a document.
 84
 85    Attributes:
 86        id: Unique note ID.
 87        note: Text content of the note.
 88        created: Timestamp when the note was created.
 89        document: ID of the parent document.
 90        user: ID of the user who created the note.
 91    """
 92
 93    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
 94
 95    id: int | None = None
 96    note: str
 97    created: datetime | None = None
 98    document: int | None = None
 99    user: int | None = None
100
101    @field_validator("user", mode="before")
102    @classmethod
103    def _coerce_user(cls, v: object) -> int | None:
104        if isinstance(v, dict):
105            return v.get("id")
106        return v  # type: ignore[return-value]

A user note attached to a document.

Attributes: id: Unique note ID. note: Text content of the note. created: Timestamp when the note was created. document: ID of the parent document. user: ID of the user who created the note.

id: int | None = None
note: str = PydanticUndefined
created: datetime.datetime | None = None
document: int | None = None
user: int | None = None
class DocumentType(pydantic.main.BaseModel): 
 9class DocumentType(BaseModel):
10    """A paperless-ngx document type (e.g. Invoice, Receipt, Contract).
11
12    Attributes:
13        id: Unique document-type ID.
14        name: Document-type name.
15        document_count: Number of documents assigned to this document type.
16    """
17
18    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore")
19
20    id: int
21    name: str
22    slug: str | None = None
23    match: str | None = None
24    matching_algorithm: int | None = None
25    is_insensitive: bool | None = None
26    document_count: int | None = None
27    owner: int | None = None
28    user_can_change: bool | None = None
